title: guard-git-mutating regex fails to match 'git -C <path> <subcommand>' — hook bypass via standard flag
description:
File: jj/hooks/guard-git-mutating, lines 50-55.

The GIT_CMD_RE regex uses (?:-\S+\s+)* to skip git flags before the subcommand. This only handles single-token flags (e.g., --no-pager). For flags that take an argument (e.g., -C /path), the pattern consumes the flag token but not its path argument, causing the subcommand match to fail.

Verified bypass cases (all return [] = not blocked):
  'git -C /path/to/repo commit -m test'
  'git -C . checkout main'
  'git -C /worktree/path worktree add ../new-wt'
  'git -C /path --no-pager commit'

CLAUDE.md itself uses 'git -C /path' patterns making this a realistic evasion path.

Fix: change (?:-\S+\s+)* to (?:-\S+(?:\s+\S+)?\s+)* to allow one optional argument after each flag. Verified the fix correctly detects all cases while preserving --no-pager behavior.

title: guard-git-mutating hook has zero tests — critical coverage gap for security-gating hook
description: jj/hooks/guard-git-mutating is a PreToolUse security hook that intercepts mutating git commands in jj repos and returns a permissionDecision='ask' response. It has zero tests in this PR. This is the highest-criticality test gap: a broken hook silently allows mutating git commands (destroying jj repo integrity) or falsely blocks read-only git commands (breaking every CI workflow). The hook has several non-trivial untested branches: (1) jj repo detection by walking ancestor directories for .jj/, (2) GIT_CMD_RE regex parsing with flag variants (-C path, --no-pager), (3) mutating vs read-only subcommand classification, (4) output JSON shape with correct hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision field. Tests to add: test_allow_in_non_jj_repo, test_block_git_commit_in_jj_repo, test_allow_git_log_in_jj_repo, test_allow_git_diff_in_jj_repo, test_empty_stdin_exits_0, test_missing_cwd_exits_0, test_jj_detected_via_parent_directory, test_output_json_has_ask_decision, test_multiple_mutating_commands_in_one_string, test_hints_list_correct_jj_equivalents. File: jj/hooks/guard-git-mutating

title: worktree-create cleanup handler may delete pre-existing directories on collision
description: .claude/hooks/worktree-create: if worktree_path already exists before the hook runs (e.g., a leftover from a previous failed creation), the VCS command (jj workspace add or git worktree add) will fail. The atexit _cleanup handler then calls shutil.rmtree(_worktree_path, ignore_errors=False) at line 144 — deleting data that was NOT created by this invocation. The hook sets _worktree_path = worktree_path before any creation attempt (line 203), so cleanup always targets the path regardless of whether it was pre-existing. Fix: check worktree_path.exists() before setting _worktree_path (or add a flag _worktree_path_created to track whether the hook created the directory). Only set the cleanup target after verifying the directory was newly created by this invocation.

title: run_cmd raises FileNotFoundError when binary missing; lefthook call in worktree-create lacks shutil.which guard, bypassing atexit disarm
description:
.claude/hooks/worktree_helpers.py:93 and .claude/hooks/worktree-create:265

run_cmd() calls subprocess.run() without catching OSError. The docstring says 'Never raises on non-zero exit' but subprocess.run raises FileNotFoundError (a subclass of OSError) when the executable is not found in PATH.

Critical impact: In worktree-create, the lefthook call at line 265 executes AFTER _workspace_created=True and BEFORE _cleanup_registered=False (line 271). If lefthook binary is missing, subprocess.run raises FileNotFoundError — an unhandled exception that bypasses the _cleanup_registered=False disarm.

The atexit handler then fires with _cleanup_registered=True and _workspace_created=True, calling jj workspace forget or git worktree remove --force — deleting the successfully-created worktree. The worktree was created correctly; lefthook failure was already a WARNING. This is spurious destructive cleanup.

The existing guard (line 264) only checks if lefthook.yml config exists, NOT that the lefthook binary is installed.

Fix: Add shutil.which guard:
    if (repo_root / 'lefthook.yml').is_file():
        if shutil.which('lefthook') is None:
            print('WARNING: lefthook.yml found but lefthook not installed', file=sys.stderr)
        else:
            lh_result = run_cmd(['lefthook', 'install'], cwd=worktree_path)
            if lh_result.returncode != 0:
                lh_err = sanitize_for_output((lh_result.stderr or '').strip()[:500])
                print(f'WARNING: lefthook install failed: {lh_err}', file=sys.stderr)

title: CI bats job attempts to run Python pytest files with bats
description:
In .github/workflows/check-skills.yml, the 'bats' CI job is configured to run 'bats --jobs 2 .claude/hooks/tests/' but all test files in that directory are Python pytest files (.py), not bats test files (.bats). bats will either error out (no .bats files found) or, depending on version, silently skip them -- in either case the pytest suite never runs in CI.

The tests are: test_post_edit_format.py, test_worktree_create.py, test_worktree_helpers.py, test_worktree_remove.py -- all standard pytest.

Fix: Replace the bats job with a pytest job. Example:

  pytest:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd
      - name: Install uv
        run: curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
      - name: Run hook tests
        run: uv run --with pytest pytest .claude/hooks/tests/ -v

title: CI bats job silently skips all Python pytest tests — no pytest runner configured
description: The CI workflow at .github/workflows/check-skills.yml (line 53-66) installs bats and runs 'bats --jobs 2 .claude/hooks/tests/'. However, .claude/hooks/tests/ contains only Python pytest files (*.py): test_post_edit_format.py, test_worktree_create.py, test_worktree_helpers.py, test_worktree_remove.py. Bats ignores files without a .bats extension, so the entire test suite silently passes without running any test. There is no pytest or 'uv run pytest' step anywhere in CI. This means every hook behavior test — jj workspace creation, git worktree lifecycle, atexit cleanup, validate_safe_name, detect_repo_root — is completely unverified by CI. A regression in any hook would not be caught before merge. Fix: replace the bats job with a pytest job (e.g., 'uv run pytest .claude/hooks/tests/ -v') or add uv/Python setup and run pytest alongside bats.

title: vcs-detection-preamble.md: 'none' VCS path prints to stdout not stderr, may corrupt hook output
description: Retracted: The STATUS: FAILED echo in vcs-detection-preamble.md is agent output consumed by orchestrators, not shell hook stdout piped to path consumers. The shell hooks use worktree-helpers.sh, not the preamble. The stdout pattern is intentional for the Claude agent protocol.

title: worktree-remove.sh: jj workspace list grep uses worktree-${WORKSPACE_NAME}: pattern but workspace name uses NAME not WORKSPACE_NAME
description: VERIFIED: jj workspace list output format confirmed as 'workspace-name: <change-id> ...' — the colon IS present. The grep pattern 'worktree-${WORKSPACE_NAME}:' correctly matches. This finding was a false alarm — closing.

title: _jj_ws_list captured but never used — two bats tests fail in worktree-remove.sh
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh line 133, the output of 'jj workspace list' is captured into _jj_ws_list but the variable is never read anywhere in the file. The intended pre-check (grep -qF 'worktree-${WORKSPACE_NAME}:' against the captured output) is absent, causing jj workspace forget to be called unconditionally for every workspace removal attempt.

Impact: Two bats tests in .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_remove.bats fail:
- 'jj path: warns and skips forget when workspace not in list' (~line 2610)
- 'jj workspace forget skips when workspace has bare name without worktree prefix' (~line 2634)

Both tests set up mocks where 'jj workspace forget' exits 1 without emitting 'no workspace named' text, then assert exit 0. Because the list pre-check is missing, the code falls through to the forget call, hits jj_forget_failed=true, and exits 1 -- causing both assertions to fail.

Root cause: The assignment 'elif [[ -n _ws_list_err ]] && ! _jj_ws_list=$(cd REPO_ROOT && jj workspace list 2>_ws_list_err)' captures the workspace list but the else branch immediately calls 'jj workspace forget worktree-WORKSPACE_NAME' without a grep pre-check. The variable _jj_ws_list appears exactly once in the file (the assignment at line 133) with zero subsequent reads.

Fix: Add a grep -qF pre-check in the else branch before calling jj workspace forget:
  if ! grep -qF 'worktree-WORKSPACE_NAME:' <<< _jj_ws_list; then
    echo 'WARNING: workspace not registered -- skipping' >&2
  elif ! jj_err=...; then
    ...existing error handling...
  fi

File: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh, lines 133-148
Tests: .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_remove.bats, ~lines 2610-2658
close_reason: Fixed: grep pre-check added, list-failure attempts forget, mktemp propagates failure, SKILL.md error handling added, tests/evals updated

title: address-findings/SKILL.md: conflict check code snippet has empty error handler — silently proceeds on jj log failure
description:
File: pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md, Phase 4b jj repos, step 3 (conflict check)

The conflict check code snippet contains an empty shell error handler:

  _conflict_check=$(jj log -r <change-id> --no-graph -T 'if(conflict, "CONFLICT", "OK")') || {
    # jj log failed — change may not exist after rebase
    # Clean up workspace (step 5), mark FAILED, re-queue.
  }
  if echo "$_conflict_check" | grep -q 'CONFLICT'; then

The '|| { # comment }' block does nothing — comments are not bash statements. If 'jj log -r <change-id>' fails (e.g., the change-id no longer exists after rebase or evolution tracking lost it), the error is swallowed, '_conflict_check' is empty or contains an empty string, and the grep finds no 'CONFLICT'. Execution continues past the conflict check as if it succeeded, silently integrating a commit whose conflict state cannot be verified.

The prose below the code block correctly explains the intended behavior ('If jj log returns non-zero: clean up workspace, mark FAILED, add bead comment, re-queue'), but the code example contradicts this — it silently swallows the error. LLMs executing this skill follow the code example.

Suggested fix: Replace the empty error handler with a status flag and an explicit check:

  _jj_log_failed=false
  _conflict_check=$(jj log -r <change-id> --no-graph -T 'if(conflict, "CONFLICT", "OK")') || _jj_log_failed=true
  if [[ "$_jj_log_failed" == true ]]; then
    # jj log failed — clean up workspace (step 5), mark FAILED, re-queue
    bd comments add <work-bead-id> "jj log -r <change-id> failed after rebase — cannot verify conflict state. Re-queued."
    # <cleanup step 5>
    # continue to next finding
  fi
  if echo "$_conflict_check" | grep -q 'CONFLICT'; then

This fix closes the gap between the stated intent (fail explicitly on jj log error) and the code example (silently ignore it).

title: validate_safe_name rejects hyphens in worktree names via regex but error message says hyphens are allowed
description: Wait - need to re-check. The regex in validate_safe_name is [^a-zA-Z0-9_.-] which uses a hyphen at the END of the character class, making it a literal hyphen. Names with hyphens like 'fix-worker-abc' pass correctly. This is NOT a bug — the placement is correct. Closing/deleting this finding.

title: vcs-detection-preamble.md: VCS detection uses test -d .jj but jj workspace dirs lack .jj/ in subdir
description:
In pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md (line 7401-7405 in diff), the VCS detection procedure instructs agents to run 'jj root >/dev/null 2>&1' first. This is correct -- it uses jj root to detect the VCS rather than test -d .jj.

HOWEVER, in address-findings/SKILL.md (line 7793-7794 in diff), the pre-loop verification block shows:
  jj log -r @- --no-graph -n 1   # MUST show PR bookmark parent (jj)
  jj st                          # MUST be clean (jj)

This is preceded by the comment 'if on main, stop'. But the jj equivalent for verifying the current PR bookmark context uses '@-' (parent of working copy). This is correct per the jj semantics documented in CLAUDE.md ('Use @- when verifying committed state').

The CRITICAL issue: in the vcs-detection-preamble.md, the jj worktree location verification uses a path pattern check ('*_worktrees/*') instead of a VCS command. If a user has a legitimately non-worktree path that contains '_worktrees' in its name (e.g. /home/user/my_worktrees_backup/project/), the check would incorrectly pass and not catch an agent running in the default workspace. Conversely, if the worktree directory uses a different naming convention (e.g. a future change to the hook), the check would incorrectly fail and report STATUS: FAILED.

The preamble at line 7430-7431 explicitly documents 'Do NOT rely on jj workspace list output to identify the current workspace; jj 0.39+ does not emit a (current) marker.' This is accurate, but the fallback of using path patterns is fragile and undocumented as a limitation.

File: pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md (lines 7414-7431 in diff)

Suggested improvement: Document the path-pattern fragility as a known limitation, or use 'jj workspace root' to get the workspace root path and compare against the expected pattern more precisely.

title: Race condition: .jj/ directory exists but jj binary not in PATH in worktree-create.sh
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh at line 2379-2392 (diff), the script detects .jj/ presence with 'if [[ -d "${REPO_ROOT}/.jj" ]]; then' and then checks 'command -v jj &>/dev/null'. These are two separate checks that can diverge in practice.

If .jj/ exists but jj is not installed: the script correctly errors with 'ERROR: .jj/ directory found but jj is not installed' and exits 1. HOWEVER, the EXIT trap (cleanup_on_error) has already been registered at this point, and cleanup_on_error also contains a branch: 'elif [[ -d "${REPO_ROOT}/.jj" ]]; then ... if command -v jj &>/dev/null; then ... else echo WARNING... fi'

This means cleanup_on_error runs AFTER the 'exit 1' from the 'jj not installed' check. The test at line 728-729 confirms this is tested and expected: '[[ "$output" == *"WARNING: cleanup: .jj/ found but jj not installed"* ]]'.

The concern is the OPPOSITE case: jj IS in PATH when the main body runs, creates the workspace (WORKSPACE_CREATED=true), then jj DISAPPEARS from PATH before cleanup_on_error runs (e.g., PATH is modified by an external process or a mounted filesystem is unmounted). cleanup_on_error then emits a WARNING about jj not installed instead of attempting workspace forget. This means a successfully-created jj workspace is left registered in jj's workspace metadata forever, with no cleanup and only a WARNING message that may be ignored.

The test at line 868-911 explicitly exercises this scenario ('jj path: cleanup_on_error warns jj-not-in-PATH after successful workspace add'). The test accepts this behavior as correct by asserting that the WARNING appears. However, this is a silent failure: the jj workspace metadata is permanently corrupted (orphaned workspace) with only a WARNING that agents may not surface to users.

Suggested improvement: When jj was successfully used to create a workspace (WORKSPACE_CREATED=true) but jj is no longer in PATH during cleanup, the error level should be ERROR not WARNING, and the exit code should reflect this more severe failure. The orphaned workspace requires manual remediation with 'jj workspace forget'.

title: address-findings SKILL.md: jj checkout flow uses 'jj new <pr-bookmark>' but jj new takes a revision, not a bookmark name
description:
File: pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md, Phase 1 step 3 (diff lines 7665-7669).

The jj checkout verification instructions say:

  - jj: Verify the PR bookmark exists: 'jj bookmark list | grep <pr-branch-name>'.
    If not found, run 'jj git fetch', then 'jj new <pr-bookmark>'.
    Verify with 'jj log -r @- --no-graph -n 1'.

The command 'jj new <pr-bookmark>' is incorrect for the stated purpose. 'jj new' creates a new empty commit on top of the given revision. After 'gh pr checkout', in a colocated jj repo, the git checkout has already moved the working copy — jj should see the PR's commits. The correct jj analog for 'checking out a bookmark' is 'jj edit <bookmark>' or 'jj new <bookmark>@<remote>' to start working on top of it.

More critically: 'jj new <pr-bookmark>' would create a NEW empty commit on top of the PR bookmark, advancing the working copy past the PR's current tip. This is the opposite of what is wanted — the orchestrator needs to be AT the PR's tip (not one commit past it) before dispatching fix agents that will rebase their changes onto @- (the PR tip).

The correct flow in jj after 'gh pr checkout' in a colocated repo should be:
1. 'jj git fetch' to sync
2. 'jj bookmark list | grep <pr-branch-name>' to confirm the bookmark exists
3. Verify with 'jj log -r <pr-bookmark> --no-graph -n 1' (not @-)
4. If needed: 'jj edit <pr-bookmark>' to check out the bookmark's tip

The use of '@-' in the verification step ('jj log -r @- --no-graph -n 1') is also wrong here — in jj, '@' is the working copy (empty) and '@-' is the parent (the last committed change). After 'gh pr checkout' in colocated mode, '@-' would show the PR tip correctly. But after 'jj new <pr-bookmark>', '@-' would show the PR tip and '@' would be a new empty commit — which is incorrect state for dispatching fix workers.

Suggested fix: Replace 'jj new <pr-bookmark>' with 'jj edit <pr-bookmark>' and update the verification step to use 'jj log -r <pr-bookmark> --no-graph -n 1'.

title: address-findings: PARTIAL/FAILED results from fix-worker omit worktree cleanup
description: pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md Phase 4b 'STATUS: PARTIAL handling' (lines 290-299): the skill instructs to skip integration and re-queue, but gives NO cleanup instruction for the worktree/workspace left behind by fix-worker. Similarly, the VCS-inference FAILED path at line 275 ('mark FAILED and skip integration') has no cleanup. The fix-worker ran with isolation: worktree, so a live workspace and directory exist. Without cleanup: (1) jj workspace metadata accumulates stale entries in the op store; (2) the sibling _worktrees/ directory grows unboundedly; (3) subsequent agents dispatched to the same name could collide. For git, the worktree registration in .git/worktrees/ also leaks. Fix: add to the PARTIAL handling section (parallel to the FAILED paths in git and jj repo sections): git: 'git worktree remove ../<repo>_worktrees/<worktree-name>'; jj: 'jj workspace forget worktree-<name> && rm -rf ../<repo>_worktrees/<worktree-name>'.

title: worktree-create.sh: cleanup_on_error trap installed before WORKTREE_PARENT mkdir, references unset WORKTREE_PATH on very early failure
description:
File: .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, lines 29-66.

The cleanup_on_error EXIT trap is installed at line 66, but WORKTREE_PATH is set unconditionally at line 27 (so it is always defined). WORKTREE_PARENT is also set at line 26. This is not a variable-unset bug.

However, there is a logic error: the trap on line 66 is installed immediately after the function definition, and the trap always invokes 'rm -rf "$WORKTREE_PATH"' on line 61, even if mkdir for WORKTREE_PARENT has not yet been called and WORKTREE_PATH does not exist. rm -rf on a non-existent path exits 0, so this is harmless in practice. BUT: the trap also calls 'cleanup_empty_parent "$WORKTREE_PARENT"' on line 64, which will attempt to rmdir the newly created parent directory if it exists and is empty. This means:

1. mkdir -p WORKTREE_PARENT succeeds (line 68)
2. jj workspace add fails (line 92-96)
3. The exit triggers cleanup_on_error
4. jj workspace forget is attempted (line 40)
5. rm -rf WORKTREE_PATH is run (line 61) — WORKTREE_PATH was never created if jj workspace add didn't create it partially, so this is a no-op
6. cleanup_empty_parent WORKTREE_PARENT runs (line 64) — correctly removes the empty parent

This IS the intended behavior and is tested. The critical observation: if jj workspace add creates the directory (partially) and then fails, the EXIT trap fires, jj workspace forget is attempted, and rm -rf WORKTREE_PATH cleans up the directory. This is correct. No actual bug here.

Correction: After further analysis this finding does not reach 80 confidence. Retracting.

title: jj workspace root is not a real jj command — VCS isolation detection will fail
description: pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md (Step 2) and jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md (Workspaces section) both reference 'jj workspace root' as a command to get the current workspace working-copy path for isolation verification. This command does not exist in jj. The jj workspace subcommands are: add, forget, list, update-stale. There is no 'workspace root' subcommand. When agents run '_ws_root=$(jj workspace root 2>/dev/null) || true', the variable is always empty because jj errors with unknown subcommand. The comparison _ws_root == _repo_root always evaluates empty == non-empty = false, silently passing every agent through the default-workspace safety check. The fallback to pwd pattern matching becomes the de facto behavior but is described as less reliable. Fix: replace 'jj workspace root' with 'pwd' to get the current workspace directory, or make the path pattern check primary.

title: Silent pass in jj workspace isolation check when 'jj workspace root' fails
description:
File: pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md, jj workspace isolation check (Step 2)

The snippet uses:
  _ws_root=$(jj workspace root 2>/dev/null) || true
  _repo_root=$(jj root 2>/dev/null) || true
  if [[ "$_ws_root" == "$_repo_root" ]]; then

The '|| true' on each line suppresses all failures. When 'jj workspace root' fails (command not found, unknown subcommand, error), _ws_root is empty. The comparison becomes '' == '/some/repo/path' which is false, so the check silently PASSES and the agent proceeds operating in an unknown workspace state.

This is a critical silent failure: agents skip isolation verification entirely and may operate in the default workspace (equivalent to main), corrupting the repository working tree.

Suggested fix: test for empty/failed output and treat it as a verification failure, then fall back to pwd pattern check as the preamble already documents but does not wire up:
  _ws_root=$(jj workspace root 2>/dev/null)
  _ws_root_ok=$?
  if [[ $_ws_root_ok -ne 0 || -z "$_ws_root" ]]; then
    # Fall back to pwd pattern (documented in preamble but currently unwired)
    case "$(pwd)" in *_worktrees/*) ;; *)
      echo 'STATUS: FAILED -- jj workspace root unavailable and pwd is not under _worktrees/'
      exit 1
    ;; esac
  elif [[ "$_ws_root" == "$_repo_root" ]]; then
    echo 'STATUS: FAILED -- Operating in default workspace'
    exit 1
  fi

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: finding is stale — the jj workspace root + || true code at vcs-detection-preamble.md was replaced by pwd-P + case pattern in fix for .4. The new code has no silent-pass issue.

title: detect_repo_root: jj_out referenced outside its local scope
description: In .claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh, the detect_repo_root function declares jj_out as a local variable inside the 'if command -v jj' block (line 55), but references it on line 62 in the error message outside that block. When jj is not in PATH, the jj block is never entered, so jj_out is unset at line 62. Under set -u (which worktree-remove.sh sets via set -euo pipefail), referencing an unset variable is a fatal error — the script crashes instead of printing the intended error message. Fix: declare 'local jj_out="" jj_rc=0' before the if block at function scope, then assign inside the block without re-declaring.

title: create_failing_logging_jj_mock uses single-quoted heredoc preventing ${REPO_ROOT} expansion
description:
helpers.bash:150 — create_failing_logging_jj_mock writes the mock script with `<< 'MOCK'` (single-quoted heredoc), causing `${REPO_ROOT}` to be written as a literal string rather than expanded to the actual temp directory path.

The mock attempts to log to `${REPO_ROOT}/forget-called.log` but at runtime the bash script contains the literal text `${REPO_ROOT}` as the filename, so it silently creates (or fails to create) a file with that literal name in the wrong place.

Compare to create_logging_jj_mock (helpers.bash:92) which correctly uses unquoted `<< MOCK` so ${REPO_ROOT} expands at mock-write time.

This affects test_worktree_create.bats:138 (`[ -f "${REPO_ROOT}/forget-called.log" ]`).

Fix: Change `<< 'MOCK'` to `<< MOCK` on helpers.bash:150 (same pattern as create_logging_jj_mock).

title: test_worktree_create.bats:132-139 broken: forget-called.log assertion can never pass
description:
The test at test_worktree_create.bats:132-139 ('jj path: cleanup_on_error calls jj workspace forget on workspace add failure') has two compounding defects that guarantee it will never pass — but will also never correctly assert the behavior under test.

**Bug 1 — Wrong heredoc quoting in create_failing_logging_jj_mock (helpers.bash:150)**
The mock uses `<< 'MOCK'` (single-quoted). This means `${REPO_ROOT}` on line 159 is written literally as the string `${REPO_ROOT}` into the mock script, not expanded to the actual temp directory path. When the mock runs, bash tries to write to a file literally named `${REPO_ROOT}/forget-called.log`, which fails silently. The log file is never created at the expected path. Compare to create_logging_jj_mock (helpers.bash:92) which correctly uses unquoted `<< MOCK` allowing ${REPO_ROOT} to expand.

**Bug 2 — WORKSPACE_CREATED guard prevents forget call on workspace-add failure**
In worktree-create.sh, WORKSPACE_CREATED is set to true (line 82) ONLY after jj workspace add SUCCEEDS. When workspace add fails (exit 1 at line 80), WORKSPACE_CREATED stays false. cleanup_on_error (line 31-50) skips the jj workspace forget branch entirely when WORKSPACE_CREATED is false (line 35 guard). The test sets up a mock that fails on workspace add — so forget is never called, and forget-called.log is never created.

**Resulting assertion failure:** `[ -f "${REPO_ROOT}/forget-called.log" ]` will always fail.

**What behavior is actually untested:** The cleanup_on_error jj workspace forget path (worktree-create.sh:38) is dead code under current logic — it would only fire if workspace add succeeded (WORKSPACE_CREATED=true) AND something between lines 82 and 92 then caused an exit. Nothing in that range exits with error today. Either the test needs to be fixed to test the correct failure scenario, or the implementation logic needs a partial-success scenario where forget is called.

**Fix:** Either (a) remove the test as it tests a scenario the implementation intentionally does not support, or (b) restructure so the test simulates a post-add failure (e.g. lefthook install failing with exit) that triggers cleanup with WORKSPACE_CREATED=true, and fix the heredoc quoting.

title: Failing test: cleanup_on_error calls jj workspace forget on workspace add failure
description: Test 'jj path: cleanup_on_error calls jj workspace forget on workspace add failure' in .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_create.bats (line 132-139) fails against the current code. The test expects forget-called.log to be created, but the WORKSPACE_CREATED guard in worktree-create.sh (line 35) prevents jj workspace forget from being called when workspace add fails -- WORKSPACE_CREATED stays false because line 82 (WORKSPACE_CREATED=true) is only reached on success. Running 'bats .claude/hooks/tests/' locally confirms: 'not ok 15 jj path: cleanup_on_error calls jj workspace forget on workspace add failure'. This is a broken test causing CI bats job to fail. Fix options: (A) Change the test to use a mock where workspace add succeeds first then a later step fails (WORKSPACE_CREATED=true path), or (B) Remove the test since the scenario it tests (forget called when WORKSPACE_CREATED=false) is contradicted by the guard's design intent.

title: vcs-detection-preamble: test -d .git fails in git worktrees — all agents would detect VCS=none
description:
File: pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md, line 8

The VCS detection command uses 'test -d .git' to check for git. In git worktrees, .git is a FILE (containing 'gitdir: /path/to/main/.git/worktrees/...'), not a directory. So 'test -d .git' returns false in every agent worktree, regardless of VCS.

Combined with 'test -d .jj' also being false in jj workspaces (since .jj/ is only in the main repo root, not in workspace directories), ALL 11 worktree-isolated agents would report VCS=none and immediately STOP in both git and jj repos.

This is a functional regression introduced by replacing the previous inline environment check (which used 'git branch --show-current') with the preamble reference.

Reproduction: Create a git worktree via 'git worktree add /tmp/test-wt -b test HEAD'. Inside /tmp/test-wt, run 'test -d .git && echo dir || echo file'. The .git entry is a file, so test -d returns false.

Fix: Use 'git rev-parse --git-dir 2>/dev/null' instead of 'test -d .git' to detect git in both main repos and worktrees. For jj: 'jj root 2>/dev/null' can detect if running in a jj workspace. The preamble should be updated to use these more robust detection methods.

title: address-findings SKILL.md: pre_rebase_parent captured too late when jj rebase commits with conflicts
description: In pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md, Phase 4b jj integration instructions tell the orchestrator to capture pre_rebase_parent IMMEDIATELY BEFORE jj rebase. However, the CLAUDE.md and MEMORY.md note for wlp.70 mentions 'move pre_rebase_parent capture before jj rebase command' was a fix applied in commit d56d533 — but looking at the diff in Phase 4b of address-findings/SKILL.md, the capture IS shown before the rebase. What is still a problem: the conflict detection logic reads post_rebase_parent AFTER a failed rebase and compares to pre_rebase_parent. If jj rebase fails with a hard error (not a conflict), the distinction logic (post_rebase_parent \!= pre_rebase_parent → conflict vs == → hard failure) could be unreliable if jj rebase partially modifies the op log before failing. The instructions say 'If post_rebase_parent == pre_rebase_parent (hard failure — nothing committed): Do NOT run jj undo', but this check uses shell string comparison between two captured values — if either capture command fails silently (e.g., jj log returns empty due to a broken state), the comparison defaults to equal and the undo is incorrectly skipped even after a conflicting commit. Suggested fix: check exit code of the post_rebase_parent capture command and handle empty/error output as an indication to run jj undo defensively rather than skipping it. File: pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md, Phase 4b Jj repos section.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: finding is stale — the ordering issue was fixed by commit d56d533. The remaining comparison reliability concern is fully subsumed by finding .13 (root cause: @- doesn't change with jj rebase -r).

title: jj conflict markers omit backslash separator — agents may fail to fully resolve conflicts
description:
File: jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md, lines 288-293 (Conflict Handling section)

The conflict marker description lists three markers but omits the fourth: the backslash separator that appears between the diff header and the 'to' snapshot section.

Actual jj 0.39 conflict format (verified by creating a real conflict):

  <<<<<<< conflict 1 of 1
  %%%%%%% diff from: <change-id> <commit-id> "description"
  \\\\        to: <change-id> <commit-id> "description"
  -base-content
  +from-a-content
  +++++++ <change-id> <commit-id> "description"
  from-b-content
  >>>>>>> conflict 1 of 1 ends

The SKILL.md only documents:
  - <<<<<<< / >>>>>>> (conflict boundaries)
  - %%%%%%% (diff-style changes)
  - +++++++ (snapshot of other side)

Missing: the backslash (7-backslash: \\\\) separator line marking the 'to:' target in the diff section. An agent following the documented markers would not recognize this line and could leave it intact after 'resolving' a conflict, corrupting the output file.

Additional omissions also worth fixing: markers include commit references in the header (e.g., '%%%%%%% diff from: noyrzntw 1d07f3e3 "base"'), and >>>>>>> ends with ' ends' suffix.

Suggested fix: Add a fourth bullet for the backslash separator line and note that all markers include commit references in the header.

title: jj undo twice on bookmark-set failure will over-roll-back: failed ops are not recorded in jj op log
description:
File: pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md, lines 354-370

The skill instructs the orchestrator to run 'jj undo' twice when 'jj bookmark set' fails, claiming 'jj records ALL operations including failures'. This is factually wrong. jj's operation log records only *successful* operations. A failed 'jj bookmark set' produces no op-log entry. Running 'jj undo' twice after a bookmark-set failure will therefore undo the rebase AND the operation before it (e.g., the previous fix's rebase from the same batch), silently rolling back already-integrated work.

The correct behavior is a single 'jj undo' to revert the successful rebase, same as the rebase-failure path (step 1 of the same section).

Suggested fix: Remove the first 'jj undo' block and the comment claiming failed ops are recorded. Replace with a single 'jj undo' call matching the rebase-failure rollback path. Update the comment to say 'jj only records successful operations; one undo reverts the successful rebase'.
close_reason: Fixed: single jj undo for bookmark-set failure (op log only records successes), all verification commands use @- instead of @

title: jj workspace list output format documented as having (current) marker does not exist in jj 0.39.0
description:
jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md lines 214-215 and pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md step 2 (jj path) both document that jj workspace list produces output in format '<name>: <commit summary>' with '(current)' appended to the active workspace line. Verified against jj 0.39.0: actual output is 'default: rwoumssn 094ee48c (empty) (no description set)' with no (current) marker. The marker does not appear in default output and cannot be produced via available templates. jj docs describe workspaces as appearing as '<name>@' in jj log, not in workspace list.

Impact: vcs-detection-preamble.md instructs agents to find the line ending with (current) and extract the workspace name -- this grep will never match, causing workspace identity verification to silently fail. Agents cannot confirm they are in a worktree workspace vs the default workspace, defeating the safety check designed to prevent accidental mutation of the main workspace.

Suggested fix for detection: use 'jj workspace root' to get the current workspace root path, then cross-reference with jj workspace list output by matching paths or deriving names from the worktree directory structure. Both SKILL.md and vcs-detection-preamble.md need updated format description and corrected detection logic.

title: jj undo recovery contradicts latest design plan: single vs double undo
description: Duplicate of fzymgc-house-skills-2d2.26 which covers the same jj undo semantic issue from the types aspect. Closing to avoid duplicate tracking.

title: Incorrect jj undo count for failed bookmark set in address-findings SKILL.md
description:
address-findings SKILL.md line 349-357 (final state after PR) still contains factually incorrect recovery instructions for jj bookmark set failure. The text says: 'Run jj undo once — this reverts the rebase (jj does NOT record failed commands in its operation log, so there is no bookmark-set operation to undo).'

This is factually wrong. jj records ALL operations including failures in the operation log. A failed jj bookmark set DOES create an entry, so recovering requires TWO jj undo calls: one for the failed bookmark set and one for the preceding rebase.

The PR's own turn-3 fix plan (docs/plans/2026-03-08-pr31-turn3-review-fixes.md Task 1) explicitly documents the correct two-undo fix and was supposed to be applied. The plan was NOT applied — the SKILL.md still has the single-undo version.

Practical impact: When jj bookmark set fails after a rebase, the orchestrator runs one jj undo (reverts only the bookmark set attempt) but does NOT undo the rebase, leaving the change silently stranded in a partially-integrated state.

File: pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md lines 349-357

Fix: Replace single jj undo with two jj undo calls (revert bookmark set, then revert rebase).

title: address-findings: jj undo twice is wrong when bookmark set fails
description:
File: pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md lines 341-350

The Phase 4b jj integration section instructs, when jj bookmark set fails:
  'Run jj undo twice — once to revert the failed jj bookmark set (jj records all operations, including failures), and once more to revert the preceding jj rebase'

This is factually incorrect. jj does NOT record failed operations in the op log. A failed 'jj bookmark set' creates no op log entry.

Verified: running 'jj bookmark set my-bm -r nonexistent-change-id' with a bad revision fails with 'Error: Revision does not exist' and 'jj op log' shows no new entry.

Impact: When bookmark set fails and the agent runs 'jj undo' twice:
  - First undo: correctly reverts the jj rebase
  - Second undo: incorrectly reverts some EARLIER operation (could be the checkout or fetch), leaving the repo in a wrong state

Fix: Remove the double-undo for the bookmark set failure case. A single 'jj undo' is sufficient to revert the preceding rebase when bookmark set fails without being recorded. Update the comment to: 'Run jj undo once — only the rebase was recorded, since jj does not log failed operations.'

title: vcs-detection-preamble.md documents wrong jj workspace list output format
description:
File: pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md lines 12-15

The preamble documents jj workspace list output as '<name>: <path>' with '(current)' marking the active workspace.

Actual output from jj 0.39.0: 'default: lmkurmoq a8721d30 (empty) (no description set)'

Format is '<name>: <change-id> <commit-id> <description>'. There is no '(current)' marker and no path. Output is identical from all workspaces.

Impact: Agents following the documented procedure (find line containing '(current)', extract name before ':') will always fail to identify their current workspace. The safety check ('verify name starts with worktree-') never executes correctly.

Verified by running jj workspace list in a repo with two workspaces. Output is same from both directories.

Fix: Remove the '(current)' marker approach. Use pwd-based workspace identity check. In jj, 'jj log -r @ --no-graph -T change_id.short(8)' gives the working-copy change-id; cross-reference with 'jj workspace list' output to find which workspace name matches that change-id. Or require agents verify via 'jj workspace list | grep "^worktree-"' (present = safe, absent = in default).

title: Cryptographic credential key committed to version control
description: A binary file named .beads-credential-key containing cryptographic key material has been committed at .beads/dolt.20260308T145118Z.corrupt.backup/.beads-credential-key. The file contains raw binary data consistent with a private/symmetric key. This file is NOT excluded by any .gitignore pattern — neither .beads/.gitignore nor the root .gitignore covers dolt.*.corrupt.backup/ directories or the .beads-credential-key filename. This file is introduced as a new tracked file in this PR (confirmed via git diff origin/main). Anyone with repo read access can extract this key. Remediation: (1) Remove file from git history with git filter-repo or BFG, (2) Rotate/invalidate the compromised key, (3) Add dolt.*.corrupt.backup/ and .beads-credential-key patterns to .beads/.gitignore.

title: address-findings.md jj bookmark set undo comment is factually wrong
description:
File: pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md (Phase 4b jj section, bookmark set failure block).

The comment says: 'A failed jj bookmark set does not create an operation log entry — jj only records successful mutations — so jj undo targets the preceding rebase.'

This is factually wrong. jj records ALL operations in its operation log, including failed ones. After a bookmark set failure, jj undo would revert the failed bookmark set — NOT the preceding rebase. To revert the rebase, a second jj undo is required.

Impact: operators following this instruction will run only ONE jj undo when they need TWO, leaving the rebase in place and putting the PR bookmark in an incorrect state.

The fix plan (docs/plans/2026-03-07-pr31-review-findings-fix.md Task 5, Step 1 for xpy.3/xpy.36) prescribed the correct two-undo behavior, but the fix was not applied — only the incorrect explanatory comment was added.

Suggested fix: Replace the failure handler to say 'Run jj undo twice — first undo reverts the failed bookmark set attempt, second undo reverts the rebase' and remove the incorrect claim about failed operations not being recorded.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text:
Closed: finding is factually incorrect. Verified by testing in a scratch jj repo:

1. Created a repo with commits + bookmark
2. Successfully rebased a fix commit onto the bookmark
3. Attempted `jj bookmark set` backwards (fails with exit 1)
4. Op log after failed bookmark set: NO new entry — top entry still 'rebase'
5. Single `jj undo` correctly reverted the rebase to pre-rebase state

Conclusion: jj op log records only successful state-modifying operations. A failed `jj bookmark set` leaves no op-log entry. The current code ('one undo targets the successful rebase') is correct. The finding's claim that 'jj records ALL operations including failed ones' is wrong.

Test env: jj 0.28+ on macOS, colocated git repo.

title: jj skill: gate op restore/abandon and add recovery ladder
description: Update jujutsu skill to make jj op restore and jj op abandon require explicit user approval, document the multi-workspace blast-radius why, and add a recovery ladder (--at-op inspect → undo → op revert → ask user). Triggered by an incident where a concurrent agent's jj op restore in another workspace silently lost edits in this workspace via update-stale recovery.
design: Option B from brainstorm: tight class gate on op restore + op abandon. Promotes op revert as default surgical recovery. Adds two undo traps (post-merge, post-push). Out of scope: multi-workspace coordination doctrine (separate bead), guard-jj-mutating hook (separate bead).
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md, jj-reference.md, and CHANGELOG.md updated; recovery ladder + MUST NOT gate present; rumdl clean; spec doc committed under docs/plans/
notes: Task 1 (SKILL.md edit) DONE: spec-compliant, rumdl clean, quality reviewed and approved. Sub-bullet indent 3-space (CommonMark canonical). Spec reviewer flagged 4-space mismatch — overruled (false positive, both render identically). Proceeding Task 2.

title: test_subdirectory_detection allows empty output (hook silently fails)
description:
In jj/hooks/tests/test_session_start_jj_detect.py, TestEdgeCases.test_subdirectory_detection (line 113) has this assertion:

  assert "jj" in result.stdout.lower() or result.stdout.strip() == ""

The disjunction permits the hook to produce empty output and still pass. This makes the test vacuous: a totally broken hook that produces no output at all satisfies result.stdout.strip() == "". The test is supposed to verify that the hook 'should detect the jj repo from the parent', but the OR branch silently allows failure.

Fix: Remove the fallback empty-output branch. The assertion should be:
  assert "jj" in result.stdout.lower()

If the hook fails to detect jj in a subdirectory, it should produce output (that is the tested behavior). An empty result.stdout in this scenario is a hook bug, not a pass condition.

File: jj/hooks/tests/test_session_start_jj_detect.py, line ~120

title: test_jj_workspace_not_in_list missing exit code assertion
description:
In .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_remove.py, TestJjIntegration.test_jj_workspace_not_in_list (line ~421) creates a directory not registered as a jj workspace, then runs the remove hook. _jj_cleanup() will attempt jj workspace forget on an unregistered workspace, which fails (non-zero exit from jj). This causes _jj_cleanup to return True (jj_forget_failed), and main() returns exit 1.

The test only asserts stderr content and directory removal — never result.returncode. The test passes even when the hook exits with 1.

Two possible fixes:
  Option A: Assert result.returncode == 0 AND change _jj_cleanup to return False when the workspace was not found in the workspace list (unregistered workspace forget failure is not a metadata leak).
  Option B: Assert result.returncode == 1 to document that unregistered-workspace cleanup is a hard error (but this conflicts with the test's docstring 'still gets removed').

Option A is likely the right design: if jj workspace list shows the workspace is absent, a failed forget cannot cause a metadata leak, so jj_forget_failed should not be set.
close_reason: Fixed: _jj_cleanup now returns False for unregistered workspaces, test asserts returncode==0

title: guard-git-mutating: no test for non-dict tool_input (AttributeError bug from prior finding still uncovered)
description:
The prior finding fzymgc-house-skills-qpw.1 identified that guard-git-mutating crashes with AttributeError when tool_input is null or a non-dict value. The new test suite (test_guard_git_mutating.py) does not include a test for this case — only test_malformed_json (non-JSON string) and test_empty_stdin are present.

The bug remains unfixed in jj/hooks/guard-git-mutating line 66 (the try/except still only catches json.JSONDecodeError and OSError), and there is no regression test to enforce the fix once applied.

Missing test cases:
  1. tool_input is null: run_hook with input '{"tool_input": null, "cwd": "..."}' — should exit 0
  2. tool_input is a string: input '{"tool_input": "not-a-dict", "cwd": "..."}' — should exit 0

These should be added to TestEdgeCases in test_guard_git_mutating.py.

title: jj/hooks/tests not included in CI test job
description:
The CI 'test' job in .github/workflows/check-skills.yml line 67 runs only '.claude/hooks/tests/', not 'jj/hooks/tests/'. The two new test files (test_guard_git_mutating.py and test_session_start_jj_detect.py) are never executed by CI, so regressions in guard-git-mutating or session-start-jj-detect would go undetected.

Fix: change the pytest invocation to cover both test directories:
  uv run --with pytest pytest .claude/hooks/tests/ jj/hooks/tests/ -v

Or use a root-level pytest discovery configuration so both are picked up automatically.

title: guard-git-mutating: AttributeError on non-dict tool_input crashes hook instead of allowing
description:
File: jj/hooks/guard-git-mutating, line 66.

The expression data.get("tool_input", {}).get("command", "") is outside any try/except. If the JSON input contains "tool_input": null or "tool_input": "some-string" (i.e., tool_input is present but is not a dict), data.get("tool_input", {}) returns None or a string, and the chained .get() raises AttributeError.

The except clause on lines 59-64 only catches json.JSONDecodeError and OSError, so the AttributeError propagates unhandled, causing the hook to exit non-zero with a traceback. The hook is intended to exit 0 (allow the command) on any input parsing problem (per the comment on line 63).

The identical pattern in post-edit-format (line 26) correctly catches AttributeError in its except clause.

Suggested fix: add AttributeError to the catch on line 62, or move lines 66-72 inside the try block:

    try:
        raw = sys.stdin.read()
        data = json.loads(raw) if raw.strip() else {}
        command = data.get("tool_input", {}).get("command", "")
        cwd = data.get("cwd", "")
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError, AttributeError):
        sys.exit(0)

title: session-start-jj-detect writes context to stderr instead of stdout — context never injected into model
description:
File: jj/hooks/session-start-jj-detect, line 94.

The hook docstring states: 'prints context...to stderr, which becomes additional context for the session.' However, the Claude SDK SessionStart hook contract injects stdout into the model's context window; stderr is displayed to the user in the UI but does NOT become model context.

The hook writes all VCS rules via: print("\n".join(lines), file=sys.stderr)

This means the jj detection and VCS rules (the entire purpose of the hook) never reach the model — they only appear in the user-facing transcript. The hook fails its stated goal of priming model context.

Fix: change file=sys.stderr to file=sys.stdout (or use json.dump with the appropriate structured output format if the SDK requires JSON for SessionStart hooks).

title: guard-git-mutating regex fails to match git -C /path and $(git cmd) patterns
description: Two regex false-negative bugs in guard-git-mutating that tests would expose: (1) git -C /path commit — the (?:-\S+\s+)* pattern matches -C as a flag but then expects the NEXT token to be the subcommand; when the argument to -C has a space separator (git -C /path commit), the /path is consumed by \s+ but the subcommand commit is not matched because the alternation group only handles single-char flags. Verified: python3 shows matches=[] for 'git -C /path commit -m x'. (2) $(git commit) command substitution — the regex prefix includes $\(\s* but the raw string in Python does not escape $ correctly for shell contexts (it works in Python regex but the test case 'var=$(git push)' produces matches=[] because the lookbehind group does not anchor on = before $). Both patterns appear in real Claude tool calls. A test_git_dash_c_flag and test_command_substitution test case would catch these. File: jj/hooks/guard-git-mutating lines 50-55
close_reason: Duplicate of gzc.7 — same regex issue

title: session-start-jj-detect hook has zero tests — untested stderr context injection path
description: jj/hooks/session-start-jj-detect is a SessionStart hook that injects VCS context into every session. It has zero tests. Unlike the other hooks with tests, this one runs at session start and its failure modes (printing wrong context, not detecting colocated repos, subprocess timeout) could silently mislead agents. Key untested behaviors: (1) pure jj repo detection and 'pure jj' label in output, (2) colocated repo detection and 'colocated jj+git' label, (3) jj not installed path (prints warning to stderr, exits 0), (4) subprocess timeout handling for jj version and workspace list, (5) no .jj/ present = exits 0 silently, (6) falls back to os.getcwd() when cwd missing from input, (7) workspace count in output message, (8) read-only git allowed line only appears for colocated repos. The hook writes to stderr (not stdout) so tests need to check stderr content. File: jj/hooks/session-start-jj-detect

title: guard-git-mutating: unguarded json.dump to stdout silently drops ask decision on pipe failure
description:
File: jj/hooks/guard-git-mutating, line 142.

json.dump(result, sys.stdout) has no error handling. If stdout write fails (BrokenPipeError, IOError, closed pipe), the exception propagates uncaught, the hook exits with an unexpected traceback to stderr but no JSON on stdout, and the PreToolUse hook framework falls back to allowing the tool call. The net effect is the 'ask' decision is silently dropped and the mutating git command proceeds in a jj repo without user confirmation — exactly the failure mode the hook exists to prevent.

In contrast, the stdin read already handles OSError at line 62. The stdout write path has no equivalent guard.

Suggested fix:
  try:
      json.dump(result, sys.stdout)
      sys.stdout.flush()
  except OSError:
      pass  # stdout closed — allow by default (best-effort, consistent with stdin policy)

title: post-edit-format catches FileNotFoundError but not PermissionError for formatter binaries
description:
In .claude/hooks/post-edit-format (line 54), the exception handler catches only FileNotFoundError. However, subprocess.run() raises PermissionError (an OSError subclass, not FileNotFoundError) when the formatter binary exists but is not executable (e.g., chmod -x ruff). This would cause the hook to propagate an unhandled exception, exiting with a traceback instead of silently continuing.

Since the intent is best-effort formatting (the docstring says 'Exits 0 regardless of formatter success'), the catch should be broadened to OSError:

    except OSError:
        pass  # formatter not installed or not executable — best-effort, skip silently

FileNotFoundError is already an OSError subclass, so this change is strictly a superset.

title: Double-cleanup on SIGTERM in worktree-create: _cleanup() not idempotent
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-create, _cleanup() checks _cleanup_registered but never resets it to False before executing. When SIGTERM fires, _sigterm_handler (line 225) calls _cleanup() which runs the full cleanup body. Then sys.exit(1) triggers the atexit handler, which calls _cleanup() a second time. Since _cleanup_registered is still True, the second invocation re-executes the full body.

Consequences:
- jj workspace forget runs twice: first succeeds, second fails and logs a spurious ERROR/LEAKED_WORKSPACE message (because _workspace_created is still True)
- shutil.rmtree(_worktree_path) is called on an already-removed directory, triggering the OSError handler and logging a spurious WARNING
- The false LEAKED_WORKSPACE stderr line could cause operators to perform unnecessary manual cleanup

Fix: add at the very top of _cleanup(), before the existing early-return check:
    global _cleanup_registered
    _cleanup_registered = False

This makes _cleanup() idempotent — the second atexit invocation immediately returns.

title: worktree-remove: unguarded sys.stdin.read() raises unhandled IOError on broken stdin
MD013 line-length violations in AGENTS.md and address-findings/SKILL.md will fail pre-commit hook
description:
Two files newly added in this PR have MD013 line-length violations (rumdl limit: 140 chars) that will fail the pre-commit hook:

1. AGENTS.md:19 — 169 chars (shell alias examples in table)
2. AGENTS.md:51 — 168 chars (jj git push --bookmark line)
3. pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md:345 — 203 chars (jj cleanup one-liner)

Run 'rumdl check AGENTS.md pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md' to confirm. Fix: break the long lines or move inline code to a fenced block (code-blocks and tables are exempt per .rumdl.toml, but these are list item inline code, not fenced blocks).

title: jj edit uses bare bookmark name after fetch — fails for untracked remote branches
description: In pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md Phase 1 step 3, after 'jj git fetch' the PR branch is fetched as '<name>@origin' (remote-tracking bookmark), not as a local bookmark '<name>'. Running 'jj edit "$BOOKMARK"' will fail when the local bookmark does not yet exist (first-time PR checkout). Fix: use 'jj edit "${BOOKMARK}@origin"' instead, or add 'jj bookmark track "${BOOKMARK}@origin" && jj git fetch' before the edit. The jj docs confirm: untracked remote bookmarks only appear as '<name>@origin' until explicitly tracked or pulled.

title: worktree_helpers.run_cmd: only catches FileNotFoundError, leaving PermissionError and other OSError unhandled
description:
File: .claude/hooks/worktree_helpers.py, lines 76-81.

run_cmd() is documented as 'Never raises' but only catches FileNotFoundError. subprocess.run() can also raise:
- PermissionError (executable exists but is not executable)
- OSError with other errno values (e.g., EACCES, ETXTBSY)
- ValueError if invalid Popen arguments are passed (though unlikely here)

A PermissionError from a non-executable binary (e.g., lefthook installed without +x) would propagate uncaught through all call sites in worktree-create and worktree-remove, bypassing the cleanup handlers and producing an unhandled traceback.

Suggested fix: catch OSError broadly (which covers FileNotFoundError and PermissionError as subclasses) and include the exception type in the synthetic stderr for debuggability:
    except OSError as exc:
        return subprocess.CompletedProcess(
            args=args, returncode=127, stdout='',
            stderr=f'{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}'
        )

title: worktree-remove _jj_cleanup: jj-not-installed path prints ERROR but returns False causing silent exit 0 on metadata leak
description:
File: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove, _jj_cleanup() lines 47-54.

When jj is not installed but a .jj/ directory exists, _jj_cleanup() prints 'ERROR: .jj/ found but jj not installed — workspace metadata not cleaned' but then returns False. The caller (main()) treats False as success (no metadata leak) and exits 0, contradicting the printed ERROR message.

The comment on that return says 'directory removal still succeeds; metadata leak is advisory' but the docstring says 'Returns True if forget failed (metadata leak)', and the actual forget-command failure path at line 97 correctly returns True. This inconsistency means jj metadata leaks are silently swallowed at the exit-code level when jj is uninstalled.

Suggested fix: return True in the jj-not-installed branch so main() exits 1, consistent with the documented semantics and with what the explicit ERROR log message implies. The comment should also be updated to match.

title: post-edit-format: subprocess.run raises FileNotFoundError when ruff/rumdl not in PATH
description:
File: .claude/hooks/post-edit-format, lines 40-52.

The three subprocess.run() calls for ruff and rumdl have no try/except around them. subprocess.run() raises FileNotFoundError (a subclass of OSError) when the executable is not found in PATH — capture_output=True does NOT suppress this exception. On a machine without ruff or rumdl installed, any Python or Markdown edit would crash the hook with an unhandled traceback rather than exiting cleanly.

The docstring says 'Exits 0 regardless of formatter success (best-effort)' but the implementation does not fulfil this contract.

Suggested fix: wrap each subprocess.run() call in try/except (OSError, FileNotFoundError), or use shutil.which() to gate each call before running it, consistent with how worktree-create.py guards lefthook.

title: worktree-remove: detect_repo_root uses Path.cwd() which may return a worktree directory when hook is invoked from within a workspace
description: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove line 158: detect_repo_root(cwd=Path.cwd()). The comment at lines 154-155 says 'Use script CWD, not the worktree path, because git rev-parse from inside a worktree returns the worktree as root.' But this assumption relies on the hook always being invoked from the main repo directory. If Claude's session CWD is inside a worktree (e.g., after a cd in an agent session), git rev-parse --show-toplevel returns the worktree root, not the main repo root. For a colocated jj workspace, this means repo_root = <workspace_dir>, and expected_parent = <workspace_parent>/<workspace_name>_worktrees — which almost certainly doesn't exist, causing the hook to fail with 'ERROR: _worktrees parent directory ... does not exist'. The fallback inference path also fails because <workspace_dir>.name doesn't end in _worktrees. Fix: document that this hook requires CWD=main repo root, and/or add a defensive check: if the detected repo root is itself inside a _worktrees/ directory, fall back to inferring the main repo root.

title: worktree-remove: jj_forget_failed=True when jj not installed exits 1 despite successful directory removal
description: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove: when jj is not installed and the repo is a jj repo, _jj_cleanup returns True (jj_forget_failed=True) at line 54. The hook then removes the worktree directory successfully (line 274-283) and cleans the parent (line 296), but exits with code 1 (line 301). The exit-1 signals 'failure' to Claude's hook framework, which may surface an error to the user or retry — even though the directory was fully cleaned up. Only the jj workspace metadata in the main repo remains (requiring manual 'jj workspace forget'). The WARNING message (lines 48-53) already instructs the user what to do, so exit 0 with that warning would be the less-surprising behavior. Fix: consider returning 0 when jj is not installed but the directory was removed, upgrading the WARNING to ERROR to maintain visibility.

title: worktree-create atexit cleanup does not fire on SIGTERM/timeout
description: .claude/hooks/worktree-create registers cleanup via atexit.register(_cleanup) at line 208. Python atexit handlers only fire on normal process exit or sys.exit() — they do NOT fire when the process is killed by a signal (SIGTERM, SIGKILL). The hook has a 30-second timeout in settings.json; if Claude's framework kills the process via SIGTERM on timeout, _cleanup() never runs. Result: partially-created jj workspaces or git worktrees are leaked. The jj case is worse — an orphaned workspace entry in jj's op log without a backing directory causes 'jj workspace update-stale' requirements on every jj command in the main workspace. Fix: install a SIGTERM signal handler that calls _cleanup() then re-raises (or sys.exit(1)).

title: worktree-create cleanup prints ERROR for jj workspace forget failure but cannot change exit code — metadata leak goes undetected by caller
description:
.claude/hooks/worktree-create:83-96 (_cleanup function)

When jj workspace forget fails during cleanup (the atexit handler), the code correctly prints an ERROR to stderr (line 87). However, the atexit handler cannot influence the process exit code — sys.exit() has already been called by main() with return 1 (or the exception propagated).

The problem: the caller (Claude hook framework) receives exit code 1 and sees an ERROR in stderr about metadata leakage. But the ERROR message is mixed in with the original failure reason, making it hard for automated systems to distinguish between 'worktree creation failed cleanly' vs 'worktree creation failed AND jj metadata leaked'.

Worktree-remove correctly handles this by checking forget failure separately and returning exit 1 specifically for that case (lines 298-301). Worktree-create has no equivalent mechanism because the cleanup runs in atexit after exit code is set.

Contrast with worktree-remove which explicitly documents:
    # jj forget failure = exit 1 (metadata leak is a hard error)

The worktree-create cleanup should emit a distinct, machine-parseable message prefix when jj metadata has leaked, to make triage possible:

    LEAKED_WORKSPACE: worktree-<name>  # on its own line for grep-ability

This allows operators/orchestrators to grep stderr for LEAKED_WORKSPACE as a post-hoc signal even when the primary exit code is 1.

title: worktree-remove returns exit 0 on JSON parse failure and missing path field — silent no-op masking hook invocation errors
description:
.claude/hooks/worktree-remove:106-119

Two silent-failure paths both return exit 0:

1. JSON parse failure (lines 106-111): If the hook framework sends malformed JSON, the removal is silently skipped with return 0. The worktree is not removed and the hook signals success. The error appears only in stderr as a WARNING, which the framework may ignore.

2. Missing 'path' field (lines 113-119): If the JSON payload lacks the 'path' key, removal is silently skipped with return 0. This is a valid contract violation by the caller.

Both cases are legitimate errors, not expected no-op conditions. Return 0 signals to the Claude hook framework that the operation succeeded, preventing any retry or escalation.

Compare: worktree-create returns exit 1 for equivalent invalid input (no 'name' field, line 170-171).

The asymmetry is subtle but consequential — a misbehaving caller that sends malformed JSON to worktree-remove will never learn its requests are silently dropped.

Fix: Return exit 1 for both cases, consistent with worktree-create:
    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
        print(f'ERROR: failed to parse JSON input: {exc}', file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

    if not worktree_path_str:
        print('ERROR: no path field in WorktreeRemove input', file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

Note: The current behavior may be intentional if the framework design treats missing input as 'nothing to do' — but that should be documented in the module docstring, and the WARNING should at minimum be an ERROR to aid debugging.

title: No test for _cleanup() with jj installed but workspace forget fails
description:
The cleanup handler in worktree-create has a branch (lines 79-96) that handles jj IS installed but workspace forget fails — but no unit test exercises this path.

The branch distinguishes two sub-cases:
1. _workspace_created=True + forget fails => ERROR (metadata leaked)
2. _workspace_created=False + forget fails => WARNING (may not have been registered)

TestCleanup covers: jj-not-installed cases, git cases, no-parent-created noop, rmtree failure. The jj-installed-forget-fails paths are absent.

Suggested tests using mock.patch.object on run_cmd returning returncode=1:
- test_cleanup_jj_installed_forget_fails_workspace_created_logs_error
- test_cleanup_jj_installed_forget_fails_no_workspace_logs_warning

These would catch a regression where ERROR vs WARNING distinction breaks or the forget command uses the wrong workspace name.

title: No test coverage for colocated jj+git repo (the primary production case)
description: The jj_repo fixture in conftest.py uses 'jj git init' (non-colocated: only .jj/ present, no .git/). However, CLAUDE.md documents that the primary production use case is colocated repos (both .jj/ and .git/), and the worktree-create/remove hooks detect jj presence via '(repo_root / ".jj").is_dir()'. In a colocated repo, git is also present and detect_repo_root will succeed via git rev-parse even though jj should be used. None of the integration tests exercise the colocated code path. A regression in the ".jj/ present + .git/ present" branch would go undetected. Fix: add a colocated_jj_repo fixture (jj git init --colocate or git init + jj git init --colocate) and run the worktree-create/remove integration tests against it. File: .claude/hooks/tests/conftest.py

title: CI test job does not install jj, silently skipping all jj integration tests
description: The CI 'test' job in .github/workflows/check-skills.yml installs uv and runs pytest but never installs jj. All TestJjIntegration tests in test_worktree_create.py and test_worktree_remove.py are decorated with @pytest.mark.skipif(not JJ_AVAILABLE, reason='jj is not installed') where JJ_AVAILABLE = shutil.which('jj') is not None. On the ubuntu-latest runner, jj is not present, so every jj integration test is silently skipped on every CI run. This means the core new functionality (jj workspace add/forget, jj workspace list failure handling, jj not-installed error path) has zero automated CI coverage. Fix: add a step to install jj before running pytest, e.g. using the official install script or cargo. Alternatively mark these tests with a custom marker and fail if the skip count exceeds a threshold.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: duplicate of 5jg.1 — both report CI missing jj install

title: jj/evals/evals.json not validated by CI lint job — check-jsonschema only validates pr-review evals
description: In .github/workflows/check-skills.yml, the 'Validate evals against schema' step runs: check-jsonschema --schemafile pr-review/evals/evals.schema.json pr-review/evals/evals.json jj/evals/evals.json. This validates jj/evals/evals.json against the pr-review evals schema. However, the jj evals file uses the same schema (no jj-specific schema was created), so this is intentional. BUT: the pr-review/evals/evals.json now has a '$schema' field pointing to './evals.schema.json' (a relative path within pr-review/), while jj/evals/evals.json presumably also has a schema reference. If jj/evals/evals.json references '$schema': './evals.schema.json', that path resolves to jj/evals/evals.schema.json which does not exist. Verify that jj/evals/evals.json either omits the $schema field or references the correct relative path to pr-review/evals/evals.schema.json.

title: address-findings SKILL.md uses 'jj edit' for PR checkout but jj edit is for moving working-copy, not checking out a branch
description: In pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md, Phase 1 step 3 for jj repos uses 'jj edit $BOOKMARK' to check out the PR branch. In jj, 'jj edit <rev>' moves the working copy to an existing revision — it does NOT create a new mutable commit on top of the bookmark. For PR editing workflows, the correct approach is typically 'jj new $BOOKMARK' (creates a new change on top of the bookmark) or 'jj edit $BOOKMARK' only if you want to directly edit the bookmark's tip commit. Using 'jj edit' leaves the working copy AT the bookmark commit (@ = bookmark), so any jj commit after this will amend/replace the bookmark's commit rather than adding a new child, which is probably not the desired behavior for a PR workflow. The instruction should clarify whether the intent is to edit the bookmark tip directly or to create a new change on top of it.

title: jj subprocess calls missing --no-pager in production hooks
description:
All production jj subprocess invocations in the hook scripts omit the --no-pager flag, while test fixtures consistently use it. In environments where jj has a pager configured, hook subprocesses may hang indefinitely since capture_output=True does not provide a TTY.

Affected locations:
- .claude/hooks/worktree-create _cleanup(): ['jj', 'workspace', 'forget', ...]
- .claude/hooks/worktree-create main(): ['jj', 'workspace', 'add', ...]
- .claude/hooks/worktree-remove _jj_cleanup(): ['jj', 'workspace', 'list'] and ['jj', 'workspace', 'forget', ...]
- .claude/hooks/worktree_helpers.py detect_repo_root(): ['jj', 'root']

Test fixtures all use ['jj', '--no-pager', ...] — production code does not.

Fix: add '--no-pager' as the first argument after 'jj' in every production run_cmd call. Example:
  run_cmd(['jj', '--no-pager', 'workspace', 'add', ...], cwd=repo_root)

title: CI test job never runs jj integration tests — jj not installed in test runner
description: The new 'test' job in .github/workflows/check-skills.yml installs uv and runs pytest but does NOT install jj. All TestJjIntegration test classes in test_worktree_create.py and test_worktree_remove.py are guarded by @pytest.mark.skipif(not JJ_AVAILABLE, reason='jj is not installed'), so the entire jj integration test suite is silently skipped in CI. The VCS-aware hook code paths for jj workspace add/forget are untested in CI. Fix: add a step to install jj before running pytest, using a pre-built binary from jj-vcs GitHub releases.

title: validate_safe_name regex unintentionally allows backslash in names
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree_helpers.py at line 57, the fullmatch regex is:

  re.fullmatch(r'[a-zA-Z0-9_.\\-]+', name)

In a raw string, '\\-' is the two-character sequence '\-'. Inside a character class, '\' matches a literal backslash, and '-' at the end is a literal hyphen. So the regex accepts backslashes in names: e.g. 'foo\\bar' passes validation.

The intent (per the docstring) is to allow only alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, and underscores. The correct regex is:

  re.fullmatch(r'[a-zA-Z0-9_.\-]+', name)   # \- at end of class = literal hyphen
or equivalently:
  re.fullmatch(r'[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+', name)

A name like 'foo\\bar' passed to 'jj workspace add --name worktree-foo\\bar' or 'git worktree add' could cause unexpected behavior. While the hook uses subprocess.run (not shell=True) so there is no direct shell injection, the backslash could confuse VCS tools or produce unexpected workspace names.

Additionally, _SAFE_NAME_RE (line 42) is compiled but never referenced in validate_safe_name or anywhere else -- it is dead code. It also allows '/' which the fullmatch regex rejects, making the two definitions inconsistent.

title: No tests for jj worktree-remove path: _jj_cleanup, jj-not-installed warning, workspace-not-in-list
description: test_worktree_remove.py tests only the git removal path. The jj VCS path in .claude/hooks/worktree-remove has zero test coverage: (1) _jj_cleanup() function (lines 1549-1610) — never called from any test; (2) jj not installed path (lines 1556-1563, returns True = jj_forget_failed, exits 1) — untested; (3) jj workspace list failure path (lines 1568-1574, warns but proceeds) — untested; (4) workspace not found in list format (lines 1578-1592, two distinct warning cases) — untested; (5) jj workspace forget failure causing exit 1 (line 1807-1808) — untested. The contract that jj forget failure returns exit 1 (metadata leak = hard error) while git prune failure returns exit 0 is a critical behavioral difference with zero test coverage. Add tests using a jj_repo fixture and mock jj commands.

title: No tests for jj worktree-create path: workspace add, old-jj version detection, jj cleanup on failure
description: test_worktree_create.py (TestGitIntegration, TestSiblingDirectory) tests only the git code path. The jj VCS path in .claude/hooks/worktree-create (lines 1420-1442) has zero test coverage: (1) _is_jj=True triggers 'jj workspace add --name worktree-<name>' — no test; (2) old jj version detection (lines 1428-1435, checks for 'unexpected'/'unrecognized'/'unknown' in output when '--name' present) — no test; (3) jj not installed early-exit (line 1402-1404) — no test; (4) _cleanup() atexit handler for jj repos (lines 1259-1295) — no test for partial failure rollback. Since jj support is the primary new feature of this PR, the complete absence of jj worktree creation tests is a significant coverage gap. Add a jj_repo fixture (similar to git_repo in conftest.py) using 'jj init' and parametrize the worktree creation tests across both VCS types.

title: validate_safe_name fullmatch regex allows literal backslash in names — undocumented and untested
description: In .claude/hooks/worktree_helpers.py line 57, the fullmatch uses the raw string r"[a-zA-Z0-9_.\\-]+". In regex syntax, inside a character class [] the sequence \\ is a literal backslash (since this is a raw string, \\ = two chars: \ and \). So the regex allows backslash characters in names like 'foo\\bar'. The docstring at line 50 says only [a-zA-Z0-9_.-] are allowed — backslash is not mentioned. The unused module-level _SAFE_NAME_RE (line 42) uses r"^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]+$" (allows slash instead). Neither regex matches the documented contract. No test exercises a backslash-containing name. Test to add: validate_safe_name('foo\\bar', 'branch') should raise ValueError. Also test that the unused _SAFE_NAME_RE is either removed or confirmed intentional.

title: CI bats job runs against Python pytest files, not .bats files
description:
In .github/workflows/check-skills.yml, the 'bats' CI job (lines 2099-2112) installs bats and runs 'bats --jobs 2 .claude/hooks/tests/'. However, all test files in .claude/hooks/tests/ are Python pytest files (test_post_edit_format.py, test_worktree_create.py, test_worktree_helpers.py, test_worktree_remove.py). Bats only runs .bats shell test files, not Python files.

This means the Python hook tests never execute in CI. The bats job either silently finds no files to run (exit 0) or errors without actually running the tests. The PR adds 4 Python test files with substantial coverage of the hook behavior, but none of it is validated in CI.

Fix: Replace the bats job with a pytest job. Install uv (already used by the hooks) and run: uv run --with pytest pytest .claude/hooks/tests/ -v. Or add a pytest invocation to the existing lint job.

title: validate_safe_name regex allows backslash in names
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree_helpers.py line 57, validate_safe_name uses the regex r'[a-zA-Z0-9_.\\-]+' which allows literal backslash characters in names. The raw string \\- inside a character class means 'backslash or hyphen', so a name like 'foo\bar' passes validation, bypassing the intent to allow only alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores.

Additionally, the module-level _SAFE_NAME_RE = re.compile(r'^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]+$') at line 42 is never used anywhere — it allows slashes but not backslashes, which is inconsistent with the inline regex and the docstring.

Fix: change line 57 to re.fullmatch(r'[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+', name) (no backslash, no slash) and either remove _SAFE_NAME_RE or replace the inline pattern with it.

title: no behavioral eval for fix-worker VCS detection branching to jj vs git path
description: The VCS detection preamble in pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md is added to all 12 pr-review agents, but there is no behavioral eval testing that fix-worker (or other agents) correctly switches to the git code path when in a git-only repo (no .jj/ directory present). Existing coverage: B-FW1 tests jj repo detection, B-VCS-GIT1 tests detection in a git repo but only asserts 'reports-failed-status' and 'no-file-edits' (not VCS branching behavior). The critical missing eval is: dispatch fix-worker in a git-only worktree and verify it reports WORKTREE_BRANCH (not CHANGE_ID) and uses git commit (not jj commit). This asymmetry means that if the jj branch accidentally executes in a git-only repo (e.g., due to a preamble bug), no eval would catch it. Suggested addition: B-VCS-FW-GIT1 eval — fix-worker in git repo (no .jj/) must report WORKTREE_BRANCH format.

title: worktree-create.sh cleanup_on_error attempts jj workspace forget unnecessarily when mkdir -p fails before workspace creation
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, when mkdir -p for WORKTREE_PARENT fails (lines 93-96), the script exits 1 via the explicit || block. However, the EXIT trap (cleanup_on_error, set at line 87) fires on this exit.

In cleanup_on_error (lines 37-50), when REPO_ROOT/.jj exists and jj is available, the code ALWAYS calls 'jj workspace forget worktree-${NAME}' — even when WORKSPACE_CREATED is false and mkdir already failed. In production, this call will fail because the workspace was never registered with jj, and the mock in the test (create_mock_jj) silently returns exit 0 for any worktree-* workspace forget, masking the production behavior.

The test at lines 1265-1270 asserts that 'jj workspace forget' does NOT appear in output, but this passes only because create_mock_jj returns exit 0 for all forget calls (no WARNING is emitted). In production with a real jj installation, the unnecessary forget attempt would emit:
  'WARNING: cleanup: jj workspace forget worktree-mkdir-fail failed (may not have been registered) — run manually if needed: ...'

This warning is misleading — there was never a workspace to forget, and the user does not need to run anything manually.

The fix would be to short-circuit cleanup_on_error when mkdir failed before any workspace was created. One approach: only attempt jj workspace forget in cleanup when WORKTREE_PARENT was successfully created (i.e., add a PARENT_CREATED flag alongside WORKSPACE_CREATED).
