title: worktree-create: no test for missing 'name' key in JSON input (vs. empty string)
description:
worktree-create.sh uses jq -r '.name // empty' (line 12) to handle both absent and null 'name' fields, falling through to the empty-string check at line 14. test_worktree_create.bats:60 tests the empty-string case ('{"name": ""}') but there is no test for a completely absent name key ('{}').

The worktree-remove.sh has tests for both absent key ('{}', test_worktree_remove.bats:98) and empty string (test_worktree_remove.bats:92). The create tests are inconsistent.

While the behavior is identical at runtime (both produce the same empty NAME), having only the empty-string test means the jq '.name // empty' construct is only partially validated. A future refactor that changes 'empty' to a default value (e.g., '.name // ""') would still pass the test, masking the behavior change. A null-key test documents and locks down the intended behavior for missing input.

Suggested fix: Add '@test "rejects missing name key in JSON input"' analogous to the remove test at line 98, using echo '{}' as input.

title: worktree-remove: inferred root with .jj and jj installed has no test
description:
worktree-remove.sh lines 94-99 check: when repo root was inferred (_REPO_ROOT_INFERRED=true), if .jj exists AND jj is installed (git check fails), the code proceeds to jj workspace forget (lines 104-110). This is the path for a pure-jj orphaned worktree cleanup where the CWD has no git ancestor.

The test 'inferred root with .jj/ but jj not installed' (test_worktree_remove.bats:221) covers the case where .jj exists but jj is NOT in PATH — it verifies the warning and directory removal.

There is no test for: inferred root + .jj present + jj IS installed + jj workspace forget succeeds. This path exercises the full jj cleanup flow for orphaned pure-jj worktrees, which is one of the PR's stated use cases (the WorktreeRemove hook being VCS-aware for pure-jj repos).

Suggested fix: Add a test analogous to 'inferred root with .jj/ but jj not installed' that uses a mock jj (e.g., create_logging_jj_mock) and verifies both that the directory is removed and that workspace forget was called with the correct workspace name.

title: worktree-remove: POSIX fallback for EXPECTED_PARENT canonicalization has no failure-path test
description:
worktree-remove.sh has two separate realpath/POSIX fallback blocks: one for WORKTREE_PATH (lines 25-36) and one for EXPECTED_PARENT canonicalization (lines 79-83). The symlink-farm POSIX test ('POSIX fallback: removes worktree when realpath absent from PATH', test_worktree_remove.bats:347) exercises the WORKTREE_PATH path but also exercises the EXPECTED_PARENT POSIX fallback as a side effect.

However, the failure case for the EXPECTED_PARENT POSIX fallback (when the ${REPO_NAME}_worktrees parent directory doesn't exist and POSIX cd fails) is not directly tested with realpath excluded. The test 'errors when _worktrees parent dir does not exist' (test_worktree_remove.bats:191) tests this failure but with realpath present, so it uses the realpath code path (line 80), not the POSIX fallback (line 82).

A failure in the POSIX fallback for EXPECTED_PARENT produces 'inconsistent state' error with exit 1, which is the correct behavior. Without an explicit test with realpath excluded, a bug in the POSIX branch (line 82) could silently fail to reject traversal attempts on POSIX-only systems.

Suggested fix: In the POSIX symlink-farm test (test_worktree_remove.bats:347), add a subcase or separate test that also verifies the 'inconsistent state' error is produced when EXPECTED_PARENT does not exist, using the realpath-excluded PATH.

title: create_failing_logging_jj_mock helper is dead code — never called after test restructure
description:
File: .claude/hooks/tests/helpers.bash, lines 148-169

create_failing_logging_jj_mock() is defined in helpers.bash but is never called by any test in the suite. It was originally introduced to fix finding .62 (single-quoted heredoc preventing ${REPO_ROOT} expansion). The fix was ultimately implemented differently: the test at test_worktree_create.bats:132-150 uses create_logging_jj_mock combined with sed-based script patching (replacing 'trap - EXIT' with 'false') to simulate a post-add failure.

The create_failing_logging_jj_mock function is unreachable dead code. Per CLAUDE.md's YAGNI mandate, it should be removed.

To verify: grep -rn 'create_failing_logging_jj_mock' .claude/hooks/tests/ — only returns the definition, no call sites.

Suggested fix: Remove the create_failing_logging_jj_mock function (lines 145-169 of helpers.bash, including the comment block).

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: finding is stale — create_failing_logging_jj_mock is now used by the test added in fix for 8ho.83 (test_worktree_create.bats:134)

title: realpath failure reason discarded in worktree-remove.sh path canonicalization
cleanup_on_error in worktree-create.sh emits unsanitized VCS error output to stderr
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, the cleanup_on_error trap emits VCS error output without sanitizing or truncating it:

Line 39:  echo "WARNING: cleanup: jj workspace forget worktree-${NAME} failed — run manually if needed: ${jj_err}" >&2
Line 44:  echo "WARNING: cleanup: git worktree remove failed — run 'git worktree prune' manually if needed: ${git_err}" >&2

Both ${jj_err} and ${git_err} are passed directly to the WARNING message without the sanitize_for_output call or the :0:200 truncation that is correctly applied to ${rm_err} on line 49:
  echo "WARNING: cleanup failed for '...' : $(sanitize_for_output "${rm_err:0:200}")")

If jj or git produces output with ANSI control codes, very long lines, or C0 control characters, the cleanup warning message can contain corrupt or unreadable output.

Fix: Apply consistent treatment:
  echo "WARNING: cleanup: jj workspace forget ${NAME} failed — run manually if needed: $(sanitize_for_output "${jj_err:0:200}")" >&2
  echo "WARNING: cleanup: git worktree remove failed — run 'git worktree prune' manually if needed: $(sanitize_for_output "${git_err:0:200}")" >&2

title: Fix(.67): add test for git path cleanup_on_error warning
description: Add test: simulate post-add failure on git path (WORKSPACE_CREATED=true), mock git worktree remove to fail, verify WARNING emitted.

title: Fix(.64): add realpath-mock POSIX fallback test for worktree-remove.sh
description: Create a test that mocks realpath to fail, so the POSIX fallback (cd+pwd -P) runs even on systems with realpath. Use a PATH-prefix mock approach.

title: Fix(.65): add detect_repo_root edge case tests
description: Add tests: (1) jj root returns non-existent directory, (2) git rev-parse takes priority over jj root when both available.

title: Fix(.61+.58): restructure test to simulate post-add failure triggering jj workspace forget
description: Restructure test_worktree_create.bats:132-139 to simulate a post-workspace-add failure (WORKSPACE_CREATED=true path). Create a new mock that succeeds on workspace add but triggers exit before cleanup trap fires with WORKSPACE_CREATED=true. Also addresses .63 gap.

title: Fix(.59+.57): fix checkout hash and pin check-jsonschema version in CI
description: check-skills.yml: (1) Replace actions/checkout hash with verified v6 SHA de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd. (2) Pin check-jsonschema to a specific version.

title: Fix(.60): update vcs-equivalence.md detection description to match actual preamble
description: vcs-equivalence.md line 7 says detection checks for .jj/ directory but actual preamble uses 'jj root' command. Update to accurately describe the command-based detection.

title: Fix(.55): stop discarding validate_safe_name error in worktree-remove.sh
description: worktree-remove.sh:45 — '2>/dev/null' on validate_safe_name call discards the error message. Remove it so users see what's wrong with the name.

title: Fix(.54): capture jj root stderr in detect_repo_root for diagnostics
description: worktree-helpers.sh:54 — jj root 2>/dev/null discards error output. Capture it so the final error message can include both git and jj failure reasons.

title: Fix(.56): capture rm -rf stderr in cleanup_on_error instead of discarding
description: Fix worktree-create.sh:48 — replace '2>/dev/null || echo WARNING' with capture pattern: if \! rm_err=$(rm -rf ... 2>&1); then echo WARNING with rm_err. Match worktree-remove.sh pattern.

title: Fix(.66): change JJ-T5 and JJ-T8 eval types from triggering to negative
description: Fix jj/evals/evals.json — JJ-T5 and JJ-T8 test non-activation (expected_skill: null, skill_not_triggered assertion) so type should be 'negative' per schema, not 'triggering'.

title: Fix(.62): change heredoc quoting from single-quoted to unquoted in create_failing_logging_jj_mock
description: Fix helpers.bash:150 — change << 'MOCK' to << MOCK so ${REPO_ROOT} expands at write time. Same pattern as create_logging_jj_mock.

title: No test covers git path cleanup_on_error warning when jj workspace forget fails in cleanup
description:
worktree-create.sh:39 — The cleanup_on_error function includes a warning path when jj workspace forget fails during cleanup (the WARNING message: 'cleanup: jj workspace forget worktree-N failed — run manually if needed'). This warning path is not tested.

However, as noted in finding fzymgc-house-skills-8ho.63, this entire branch is currently unreachable. If the implementation is ever changed to make it reachable (e.g. by adding fatal post-add steps), this warning path would need a test.

Similarly, the git path cleanup warning ('cleanup: git worktree remove failed — run git worktree prune manually') at line 44 is also untested. Since the git WORKSPACE_CREATED=true path IS reachable (workspace add succeeds then something fails), this gap is more realistic for the git path if future code adds fatal post-add steps.

Suggestion: Add a test that mocks a scenario where git worktree add succeeds (WORKSPACE_CREATED=true) and then a simulated fatal subsequent step triggers cleanup, verifying the git worktree remove fallback and its warning message.

title: No test for detect_repo_root POSIX fallback (no realpath) in test_worktree_helpers.bats
description:
test_worktree_helpers.bats — detect_repo_root in worktree-helpers.sh does not use realpath, so the helper tests do not need a POSIX fallback. However, test_worktree_helpers.bats has no test for the case where jj is installed, jj root returns a non-empty result, but the result is a path that does not exist as a directory.

The existing test at line 153 tests jj root returning a file path (non-directory), but there is no test for jj root returning a path to a directory that does not exist (non-existent directory). This is a subtle gap in the detect_repo_root unit tests.

Additionally, there is no test for the case where jj is installed and jj root succeeds, but the CWD already has a git repo ancestor — verifying that git rev-parse takes precedence over jj root (the priority order in detect_repo_root:47-51 vs 53-58).

title: No test for worktree-remove.sh POSIX fallback path on systems where realpath is unavailable
description:
test_worktree_remove.bats:325-345 — The POSIX fallback test for worktree-remove.sh self-skips on any system where realpath is available ('realpath is available — POSIX fallback cannot be tested on this system'). Since ubuntu-latest CI always has realpath, this test never runs in CI.

The worktree-remove.sh POSIX fallback paths (lines 30-36 and 81-83) use `cd+pwd -P` instead of `realpath`. These paths are never exercised in CI.

Similarly, test_worktree_create.bats has no corresponding POSIX fallback test (worktree-create.sh does not use realpath, so this is less critical there).

Options to improve coverage:
1. Use a PATH-prefix mock that wraps a failing realpath binary (like the git mock pattern used elsewhere) so CI can test the fallback path without actual realpath absence.
2. Extract the realpath/fallback logic into a helper function in worktree-helpers.sh and unit-test it directly in test_worktree_helpers.bats with a mocked realpath.

This is a suggestion rather than a blocker — the POSIX fallback path is straightforward, but the current test strategy leaves it completely unverified in any automated context.

title: vcs-equivalence.md incorrectly describes detection pattern as checking .jj/ directory
description: In pr-review/references/vcs-equivalence.md line 7: 'The detection pattern (see vcs-detection-preamble.md) checks for .jj/ first, then .git/, and reports none if neither exists.' This is factually wrong. The vcs-detection-preamble.md (line 11) uses command-based detection: 'if jj root 2>/dev/null >&2; then echo jj'. It does NOT use test -d .jj. The two approaches have different semantics: test -d .jj fails in worktrees where the .jj/ directory is absent from the working directory, while jj root works in any jj workspace. The vcs-equivalence.md description is misleading and could cause agents to use the wrong approach. Fix: update vcs-equivalence.md to accurately describe the detection as using 'jj root' command, not .jj/ directory check.

title: CI check-jsonschema pip install has no version pin causing non-deterministic builds
description:
In .github/workflows/check-skills.yml line 91:

  pip install check-jsonschema

This installs the latest available version of check-jsonschema at CI runtime with no version constraint. If a breaking version is released, CI will silently start validating with different schema behavior or fail in unexpected ways without any change to the repository.

This is a latent silent-failure risk: the evals validation step could start erroneously passing or failing due to an upstream tool change, not a code change.

Suggested fix: Pin to a specific version:
  pip install check-jsonschema==0.31.3

Alternatively, add it to a requirements file or use `--constraint` to bound the version.

title: validate_safe_name error message silently discarded in worktree-remove.sh
description:
In worktree-remove.sh line 45, validate_safe_name is called with `2>/dev/null` to suppress its stderr output:

  if ! validate_safe_name "$WORKSPACE_NAME" "worktree name" 2>/dev/null; then

The function prints a descriptive error like "ERROR: invalid worktree name 'evil;rm' (alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores only; no leading dot)" to stderr, but that output is silently discarded. Only the generic WARNING about 'unusual characters' is emitted instead.

This is intentional design (lenient on removal) but the diagnostic value of the specific validation failure is lost. An operator debugging why a particular orphaned worktree name was flagged would not see which rule it violated.

Suggested fix: Capture and include the validate_safe_name error in the WARNING:
  if vn_err=$(validate_safe_name "$WORKSPACE_NAME" "worktree name" 2>&1); then :; else
    echo "WARNING: worktree name '$(sanitize_for_output "$WORKSPACE_NAME")' contains unusual characters (${vn_err}) — proceeding with removal" >&2
  fi

title: Fix(.28): Add test for tab and carriage-return preservation in sanitize_for_output
description: Add test to test_worktree_helpers.bats verifying sanitize_for_output preserves tab (0x09) and carriage-return (0x0D) characters.

title: Fix(.29): Add test for POSIX fallback path (no realpath) in worktree-remove
description: Add test that mocks realpath as unavailable via PATH manipulation and verifies the cd+pwd -P fallback produces correct canonicalization.

title: Fix(.30): Add test for inferred-root with .jj/ but jj not installed
description: Add a test to test_worktree_remove.bats for the path where _REPO_ROOT_INFERRED=true and the inferred root has .jj/ but jj is not in PATH. The script should skip VCS deregistration (skip_vcs_cleanup=true) and proceed with rm -rf.

title: Fix(.32): Add negative eval for colocated repo git-read-only operations
description: Add a behavioral eval to jj/evals/evals.json that verifies read-only git commands (git log, git status, git diff) are NOT warned against in a colocated jj repo. Existing JJ-B5 tests that mutating git commands ARE warned against.

title: Fix(.36 retry): Add jj undo verification command in address-findings step 2
description: Previous fix added undo failure handling but left 'Verify undo succeeded' without specifying the command. Add explicit 'jj log -r @- --no-graph -n 1' verification, matching step 3's pattern.

title: Fix(.36): Add jj undo failure handling in address-findings step 2
description: In pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md Phase 4b jj section step 2 (lines 366-369), add explicit handling for when jj undo fails after detecting a conflicted rebase. Should STOP and report STATUS: FAILED with manual recovery instructions, matching the pattern used in step 3.

title: Fix(.31): Add test for cleanup_on_error git path guard (WORKSPACE_CREATED=false)
description: Add a test to test_worktree_create.bats that verifies cleanup_on_error does not attempt git/jj cleanup when WORKSPACE_CREATED=false (i.e., when mkdir or VCS workspace creation fails before the flag is set).

title: Fix(.34 retry): Update worktree-remove.sh test for exit 1 behavior + error message
description: The test at line 191-207 of test_worktree_remove.bats still asserts status=0. Update it to assert status=1 and check for the ERROR message about inconsistent state. Also update the test name to reflect the new error behavior.

title: Fix(.39): Replace path pattern with jj root check in vcs-detection-preamble
description: In pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md, replace the case pwd pattern _worktrees/* check (lines 25-31) with a jj workspace root comparison or add a jj root check. Also clarify pseudo-code blocks are not directly runnable.

title: Fix(.33): Add test for absent path key in worktree-remove JSON input
description: Add a test to test_worktree_remove.bats that sends '{}' (no path key) and verifies the script exits 0 with 'no path field' in output.

title: Fix(.34): Error on EXPECTED_PARENT canonicalization failure instead of silent exit 0
description: Replace 'exit 0' with 'exit 1' + error message in the EXPECTED_PARENT canonicalization block (lines 79-83 of worktree-remove.sh). The comment claims worktree is already gone but WORKTREE_PATH was just verified to exist.

title: Fix(.37): Replace jj wildcard with specific read-only jj subcommands in review-pr
description: Replace 'Bash(jj *)' on line 27 of pr-review/skills/review-pr/SKILL.md with specific read-only jj commands: jj st, jj log, jj diff, jj show, jj file list, jj root. These match the read-only pattern used in address-findings and respond-to-comments skills.

title: Fix(.35): Fail-safe exit when repo root cannot be determined in worktree-remove
description: Replace the rm -rf fallback in the else branch (lines 65-74 of worktree-remove.sh) with exit 1. When repo root can't be determined and path doesn't match _worktrees pattern, refuse to remove rather than removing without containment check.

title: Fix(.38): Move trap - EXIT before echo to prevent workspace destruction on broken stdout
description: Move 'trap - EXIT' from line 100 to immediately after WORKSPACE_CREATED=true (after the if/else block at line 90), before the lefthook install and echo. This prevents cleanup_on_error from firing if echo fails due to broken pipe.

title: vcs-detection-preamble jj location check uses path pattern not VCS command
description: In pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md, the jj location verification step uses a path pattern match: case "$(pwd)" in *_worktrees/*). This pattern could falsely pass for a directory that happens to contain _worktrees/ in its path but is not an actual jj workspace (e.g., /home/user/my_worktrees/some-dir). The git verification step is more robust: it uses git branch --show-current and checks for a worktree/* branch name. The jj verification is weaker because jj workspaces do not have a branch-name equivalent to check. Minor documentation concern: the pseudo-code block uses STOP as a non-runnable keyword but is presented in a code block, which may confuse agents trying to execute it literally. Suggested improvement: add a comment clarifying the block is pseudo-code, or use a prose description + separate runnable snippet.

title: address-findings: jj undo failure after conflict not handled in step 2
description:
In pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md, Phase 4b Jj repos section, step 2 handles conflict detection after a successful rebase. It says:
  'If has_conflict == "true": Run jj undo to revert the conflicted rebase. Verify undo succeeded. Mark FAILED ...'

Two gaps compared to step 3 (bookmark-set failure):
1. No explicit instruction for what to do if jj undo fails — step 3 says 'If jj undo fails, STOP and report STATUS: FAILED with "jj undo failed to revert rebase — manual recovery required"', but step 2 has no equivalent.
2. No verification command specified — step 3 gives 'jj log -r @- --no-graph -n 1' as the verification command; step 2 only says 'Verify undo succeeded' without specifying how.

These gaps mean a Claude model following step 2 might silently skip the undo failure case or use an incorrect verification method.

Suggested fix: Add the same undo-failure handling and explicit verification command to step 2 that step 3 already has.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Review gate failed: fix adds undo failure handling but does not add explicit verification command (jj log -r @- --no-graph -n 1). Re-queued for round 4.

title: Missing test: worktree-remove does not test missing 'path' field (null JSON key vs empty string)
Missing eval: jujutsu skill does not have negative test for colocated repo git-read-only operations
description:
jj/evals/evals.json has JJ-B5 which tests that the skill warns against mutating git commands (e.g. git rebase) in a jj repo. However there is no eval verifying that READ-ONLY git commands (git log, git show, git diff) are acceptable to use in colocated repos. The jj skill and jj-git-interop.md reference document that read-only git commands are safe in colocated repos, but no eval tests this distinction.

Without this eval, a regression where the skill warns against ALL git commands (read-only included) would not be caught.

File: jj/evals/evals.json

Suggested addition: A behavioral eval like:
  id: JJ-B-GITREAD1
  prompt: 'run git log to see recent commits'
  context: 'Repo root contains .jj/ directory (colocated jj+git repo)'
  assertion: does-not-warn-against-git-log — skill should allow read-only git commands

Criticality: 4 — behavioral regression could cause the skill to over-warn and block legitimate read-only operations.

title: Missing test: worktree-create cleanup_on_error does not test git path cleanup guard (WORKSPACE_CREATED=false)
description:
worktree-create.sh sets WORKSPACE_CREATED=false before the mkdir and only sets it true after the VCS workspace creation succeeds. The cleanup_on_error trap checks this flag to avoid running git/jj cleanup when nothing was created. The existing tests verify: (1) jj cleanup_on_error calls jj workspace forget when workspace WAS created (WORKSPACE_CREATED=true path). But no test verifies that cleanup_on_error does NOT call git worktree remove when failure occurs before workspace creation (e.g. during mkdir -p failure). If WORKSPACE_CREATED stays false and cleanup runs, it should skip the git/jj deregistration entirely.

The mkdir-p-failure tests ('fails gracefully when mkdir -p fails') verify exit code but don't assert that no spurious git/jj error messages appear, which would indicate the guard is working.

File: .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh (cleanup_on_error WORKSPACE_CREATED guard)
Test file: .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_create.bats

Suggested improvement: The mkdir-p-failure tests should also assert the output contains no 'git worktree remove' or 'jj workspace forget' invocation errors, confirming the early-exit guard works.

Criticality: 3 — the guard prevents spurious error messages, not data loss.

title: Missing test: worktree-remove inferred-root path when inferred root has .jj/ but jj not installed
description:
worktree-remove.sh has a path where the repo root is inferred from the worktree path (lines 1641-1649 in diff). When _REPO_ROOT_INFERRED==true, it checks whether the inferred root has .jj/ or .git/. The case where inferred root has .jj/ but jj is not installed triggers the 'WARNING: .jj/ found but jj not installed' warning branch at line 1655-1656.

There is no test for this combination: inferred root + .jj/ present + jj not in PATH. The existing test 'jj path: warns but still removes directory when jj not installed' uses a real git repo (detect_repo_root succeeds directly), not the inferred-root path.

File: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh (inferred-root + jj-absent branch)
Test file: .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_remove.bats

Suggested addition:
  @test "inferred root + .jj/ + jj absent: warns and removes" {
    NON_GIT=$(mktemp -d)
    mkdir -p "${NON_GIT}/.jj"
    mkdir -p "${NON_GIT}_worktrees/orphan-jj-wt"
    PATH="/usr/bin:/bin" run bash -c 'cd '"$NON_GIT"' && echo "{\"path\": \"'"${NON_GIT}_worktrees/orphan-jj-wt"'\"})" | bash $BATS_TEST_DIRNAME/../worktree-remove.sh 2>&1'
    [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
    [[ "$output" == *"WARNING"* ]]
    [ \! -d "${NON_GIT}_worktrees/orphan-jj-wt" ]
  }

Criticality: 4 — this path exists in the production code and could leave orphaned jj workspace metadata silently.

title: Missing test: worktree-remove POSIX fallback path (no realpath) not exercised
description:
worktree-remove.sh has a POSIX fallback for path canonicalization when realpath is unavailable (lines 1574-1580 in diff). The fallback uses 'cd $path && pwd -P'. All BATS tests run on ubuntu-latest where realpath is always available, so this branch is never exercised in CI.

File: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh lines 1574-1580 (diff numbering)
Test file: .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_remove.bats

Suggested fix: Add a test that mocks realpath as unavailable (via PATH manipulation that omits it) and verifies the cd+pwd fallback produces the same result.

Criticality: 4 — the fallback only matters on minimal POSIX systems (no realpath), but if it has a bug it would silently allow path traversal bypasses on those systems.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Review gate failed: test always self-skips in CI because realpath is always available on ubuntu-latest. The finding itself acknowledges this limitation. Options: (1) accept the skip-guarded test as documentation, (2) create a Docker-based CI step without realpath, (3) defer. Escalating to user.

title: Missing test: sanitize_for_output does not test tab character preservation
Fix(.4): preserve newlines in sanitize_for_output for multi-line jj errors
description: In worktree-create.sh cleanup_on_error, the sanitize_for_output function strips all C0 control chars including newlines, making multi-line jj errors unreadable. Preserve newlines in the sanitizer.

title: Fix(.9): add eval for VCS field inference when fix-worker omits VCS field
description: Add behavioral eval to pr-review/evals/evals.json covering the VCS field inference fallback path in Phase 4 step 5.

title: Fix(.7): add detect_repo_root test for git fails + jj not in PATH
description: Add test case to test_worktree_helpers.bats for when git rev-parse fails AND jj is not in PATH (command -v jj fails).

title: Fix(.10): add checksum verification for bats installation in check-skills.yml
description: Add SHA-256 checksum verification after downloading bats-core tarball in .github/workflows/check-skills.yml, matching the pattern used for rumdl installation.

title: Fix(.3): add STATUS: FAILED handling guidance in vcs-detection-preamble
description: The preamble says agents should report STATUS: FAILED when VCS=none, but orchestrators (address-findings, review-pr) have no documented contract for handling this. Add a note about the expected orchestrator behavior.

title: Fix(.5): add eval for jj rebase failure and undo rollback in address-findings
description: Add behavioral eval to pr-review/evals/evals.json covering the jj rebase conflict detection and undo rollback logic in Phase 4b.

title: Fix(.6): add error handling for VCS field inference fallback in address-findings Phase 4
description: In Phase 4 step 5 VCS field inference, add explicit handling for when the worktree path no longer exists or jj log fails. Currently the text says 'If it fails, mark FAILED' but doesn't handle the missing-worktree case distinctly.

title: Fix(.8): add .jj/.git existence check for inferred repo root in worktree-remove.sh
description: When repo root is inferred from path string manipulation, verify the inferred path actually contains .jj or .git before using it for VCS detection. If neither exists, log a warning and skip VCS cleanup.

title: Fix(.1): guard cleanup_on_error VCS deregistration with WORKSPACE_CREATED flag
description: Add WORKSPACE_CREATED=false guard variable, set true after successful workspace creation, check in cleanup_on_error before attempting VCS deregistration.

title: Fix(.15): add jj undo to allowed-tools in jujutsu SKILL.md
description: Add 'Bash(jj undo *)' to the allowed-tools list in jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md frontmatter. The skill documents jj undo in the body but it's missing from allowed-tools.

title: Fix(.13): rewrite Phase 4b jj conflict detection
description: Replace pre_rebase_parent/@- comparison (lines 344-372) with: (1) check exit code for hard failure, (2) check jj log -r <change-id> -T conflict template for conflict detection. Eliminates @- usage entirely.

title: Fix(.11): replace test -d .git with git rev-parse in VCS detection preamble
description: Replace 'test -d .jj' and 'test -d .git' with 'jj root 2>/dev/null' and 'git rev-parse --git-dir 2>/dev/null' in vcs-detection-preamble.md. This fixes detection in worktrees where .git is a file, not a directory.

title: check-skills.yml: bats test job installs bats via tar without checksum verification
description: In .github/workflows/check-skills.yml, the new bats job installs bats-core via wget from GitHub releases with no checksum verification: 'wget -q https://github.com/bats-core/bats-core/archive/refs/tags/v1.11.0.tar.gz && tar xzf v1.11.0.tar.gz'. The rumdl installation in the same workflow was updated in this PR to include sha256 checksum verification (sha256sum -c). The bats installation follows the old pattern without integrity verification. While this is a test-only job (not production), a supply chain compromise of the bats GitHub release artifact would silently execute arbitrary code in the CI environment during test runs. Suggested fix: add SHA256 checksum verification for the bats tarball before extracting, similar to the rumdl pattern added elsewhere in the same file. File: .github/workflows/check-skills.yml, bats install step.

title: No eval for address-findings VCS field inference when fix-worker omits VCS field
description:
address-findings SKILL.md Phase 4 step 5 documents a fallback procedure: if the VCS field is missing from the fix-worker response, the orchestrator should detect VCS from the working directory, infer the identifier (branch or change-id), log a warning, and continue. This inference path is new code added in this PR and has no behavioral eval coverage.

Location: pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md, Phase 4 'Collect results' step: 'If VCS is missing from the fix-worker response'.

The missing eval should verify that address-findings correctly falls back to VCS detection when fix-worker returns an old-format response (no VCS field), rather than failing or silently using the wrong integration method.

Suggested addition to pr-review/evals/evals.json:
- B-JJ-COMPAT1: fix-worker returns STATUS: FIXED without VCS field (old format). In a jj repo, agent should infer VCS: jj, warn about missing VCS field, and proceed with jj integration.
- B-JJ-COMPAT2: same scenario in a git repo, ensure WORKTREE_BRANCH is correctly inferred.

Criticality 5/10: affects correctness during transition period when older fix-worker responses are processed.

title: detect_repo_root unit test missing: git fails + jj not in PATH error path
description:
test_worktree_helpers.bats has exactly one detect_repo_root test: it covers the case where git rev-parse fails, jj is installed but returns a file path (not a directory) -> error. This leaves a gap: when git rev-parse fails AND jj is not in PATH (command -v jj fails), the function should fall through to the error message 'not inside a git/jj repository'. This branch is untested at the unit level in .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_helpers.bats.

Production code: .claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh, detect_repo_root(), the 'if command -v jj &>/dev/null' branch when the condition is false.

The integration-level test 'fails when not inside a git repo (git rev-parse failure)' in test_worktree_create.bats covers the full-script error path in a non-.jj directory, but no test exercises the specific case: .jj exists, git fails, jj binary absent.

Suggested addition to test_worktree_helpers.bats:
  @test 'detect_repo_root: returns error when git fails and jj not in PATH' {
    run env -i HOME="$HOME" PATH="/usr/bin:/bin" \
      bash -c 'source ".../worktree-helpers.sh" && detect_repo_root'
    [ "$status" -ne 0 ]
    [[ "$output" == *"ERROR"* ]]
  }

Criticality 4/10: covered implicitly by integration tests but missing as a focused unit test.

title: worktree-create.sh: cleanup_on_error silently suppresses jj workspace forget error output in cleanup path
description: In .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh cleanup_on_error function, when jj workspace forget fails during cleanup, the warning message includes sanitized output of jj_err. However, sanitize_for_output strips ALL C0 control characters including newlines, meaning a multi-line jj error message is concatenated into a single string without any delimiter (e.g. 'Error: workspace not foundHint: workspace add must be run from the workspace root'). This loses the structure of the error message and makes debugging harder. The same pattern applies in worktree-remove.sh line 1628. Suggested fix: use a separator (e.g., ' | ') when collapsing multi-line output, or limit to only the first line using 'head -n1' equivalent: jj_err_first_line=. File: .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh cleanup_on_error function, .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh line ~1628

title: vcs-detection-preamble.md: 'none' VCS condition stops agent with STATUS: FAILED but orchestrator has no contract to handle this
description: In pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md, step 1 says: if the result is 'none', STOP and report STATUS: FAILED -- 'No VCS detected (no .jj/ or .git/ directory)'. However, the orchestrator (address-findings/SKILL.md) and the other skills have no documented handling for receiving STATUS: FAILED from a review agent due to missing VCS. The skills and agents all delegate to this preamble reference, but the failure mode is not surfaced to the user — it would silently disappear into the agent output without the orchestrator having explicit handling for it. A 'none' result is especially dangerous in worktree agents since it would mean the agent ran in a completely wrong directory, and the silent FAILED status might not stop the orchestrator's batch from proceeding. Suggested fix: Document in address-findings Phase 4 'Collect results' section that STATUS: FAILED with 'No VCS detected' must be treated as a fatal infrastructure error (not re-queued), and the orchestrator should stop the entire batch and alert the user. File: pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md + pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md

description: Add check-jsonschema validation step in check-skills.yml for evals.json files against evals.schema.json

title: Fix(wlp.70): distinguish conflict vs hard failure before jj undo
description: Capture change-id before rebase, compare after failure. Only undo if change-id advanced (conflict). Skip undo for hard failures.

description: Add jj/commands/*.md glob to rumdl check in check-skills.yml

description: Align STATUS values to FIXED | PARTIAL | FAILED across all 3 docs. Add PARTIAL handling in address-findings.

title: detect_repo_root function has no direct unit tests in test_worktree_helpers.bats
description: worktree-helpers.sh exports three testable functions: sanitize_for_output, validate_safe_name, and cleanup_empty_parent — all unit-tested in test_worktree_helpers.bats. detect_repo_root (added in this PR) is covered only via integration paths in test_worktree_create.bats and test_worktree_remove.bats. There is no direct unit test for the jj-only fallback edge case (jj root returns a non-directory path) which would silently fall through to the error message. Adding one @test to test_worktree_helpers.bats that sources worktree-helpers.sh and calls detect_repo_root with a mock jj returning a file path would complete the unit coverage for this module.

title: evals.schema.json has no CI validation step — schema drift is undetected
description: pr-review/evals/evals.schema.json and jj/evals/evals.json were both added in this PR, but .github/workflows/check-skills.yml has no step to validate evals files against the schema. A future edit to evals.json that violates the schema (wrong assertion type enum, missing required field, etc.) will pass CI silently. The schema's additionalProperties: false constraint in particular becomes useless without runtime validation. Suggested fix: add a drift-check step using a tool like check-jsonschema (pip install check-jsonschema) or ajv-cli to validate jj/evals/evals.json and pr-review/evals/evals.json against the schema on every PR.

title: CI lint step omits jj/commands/*.md — jj-init.md is not linted
description:
The 'lint' job in .github/workflows/check-skills.yml (lines 83-86) runs rumdl on:
  - homelab/skills/*/SKILL.md
  - pr-review/skills/*/SKILL.md
  - jj/skills/*/SKILL.md
  - pr-review/agents/*.md

It does not include jj/commands/*.md. The new jj-init.md command file (jj/commands/jj-init.md) is therefore never linted by CI, which contradicts the project's stated goal of enforcing markdown formatting on all skill/command files via pre-commit hooks.

Suggested fix: Add jj/commands/*.md to the rumdl check glob in .github/workflows/check-skills.yml:
  rumdl check homelab/skills/*/SKILL.md     pr-review/skills/*/SKILL.md     jj/skills/*/SKILL.md     jj/commands/*.md     pr-review/agents/*.md

title: cleanup_on_error: git_err captured but not included in warning; jj error silenced entirely
description:
File: .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, cleanup_on_error function (~lines 36-39)

The wlp.54 fix (commit 2bd9de3) added a WARNING message when git worktree remove fails in cleanup_on_error, but the captured git_err variable is never printed:

  if \! git_err=$(git worktree remove --force "$WORKTREE_PATH" 2>&1); then
    echo "WARNING: cleanup: git worktree remove failed — run 'git worktree prune' manually if needed" >&2
  fi

git_err holds the actual git error text but is discarded. This makes the warning non-actionable when debugging why cleanup failed.

Additionally, the jj branch at line 33 uses 2>/dev/null to suppress all jj output, so no error detail is available even in principle:

  (cd "$REPO_ROOT" && jj workspace forget "worktree-${NAME}" 2>/dev/null) || \
    echo "WARNING: cleanup: jj workspace forget worktree-${NAME} failed — run manually if needed" >&2

Contrast with worktree-remove.sh lines 104-105 which correctly captures and includes jj_err in the warning.

Suggested fix:
- git path: append $(sanitize_for_output "${git_err:0:200}") to the WARNING message
- jj path: capture jj error output (remove 2>/dev/null, use command substitution) and include in WARNING, matching worktree-remove.sh pattern

title: Fix(wlp.58): add jj-path lefthook integration test
description: Add test using setup_jj + create_mock_jj + mock lefthook that verifies lefthook install runs after jj workspace add.

title: Fix(wlp.57): add jj create-then-remove lifecycle test
description: Add test that exercises worktree-create.sh then worktree-remove.sh, verifying workspace name consistency.

description: Add test for worktree-remove.sh branch where detect_repo_root fails and parent dir does not end in _worktrees.

description: Update # v4 comments to # v6 for actions/checkout hash in check-skills.yml.

description: Wrap WORKSPACE_NAME with sanitize_for_output in worktree-remove.sh line 105 warning message.

description: Add setup_colocated_jj helper and colocated tests to test_worktree_create.bats and test_worktree_remove.bats.

description: Replace silenced git worktree remove in cleanup_on_error (line 37 of worktree-create.sh) with a pattern that captures and logs failures, consistent with the jj branch.

description: Add note to step 2 in pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md that location check applies only to worktree-isolated agents, not orchestrator skills.

title: Fix(wlp.51): remove early exit 1 so rm -rf is always reached
description: Drop exit 1 at line 113 in worktree-remove.sh when git worktree prune fails. Fall through to rm -rf. Keep the ERROR message.

title: actions/checkout pinned hash has incorrect version comment (says v4, is v6)
description: In .github/workflows/check-skills.yml, all three uses of actions/checkout are pinned to hash 34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 with the comment '# v4'. However, the commit at that hash has message 'Cleanup actions/checkout@v6 auth style (#2305)', indicating it is a v6 commit, not v4. This is a misleading comment that could confuse future maintainers about which version is actually being used. The comment should be updated to '# v6' to accurately reflect the pinned version.

title: No test for jj path lefthook install in worktree-create.sh
description: The lefthook integration tests in test_worktree_create.bats (lines 238-283) exercise the shared post-VCS path that runs for both git and jj. However, these tests use the git path (no .jj/ in REPO_ROOT). The comment at line 238 says 'Lefthook runs on shared post-VCS code (identical for git and jj paths), so these tests cover both code paths without duplication.' While true that the code is shared, the setup difference matters: in the jj path, the worktree-create.sh takes the jj workspace add branch first (lines 49-72), and only reaches the lefthook block if that succeeds. The lefthook tests never exercise jj workspace add + lefthook install in sequence. If a future refactor accidentally puts lefthook inside the git-only branch, these tests would still pass. Suggested fix: add one test using setup_jj + create_mock_jj + a mock lefthook that verifies lefthook install runs after a successful jj workspace add. Location: .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_create.bats

title: No eval or test covering jj worktree creation then removal round-trip
description: The bats tests for the jj hook path test create and remove as independent operations. There is no end-to-end test that creates a jj workspace via worktree-create.sh and then removes it via worktree-remove.sh in sequence, verifying that the workspace name convention (worktree-${NAME}) is consistent between both scripts and that the forget-then-rm pattern leaves no orphaned metadata. The fix-worker/orchestrator integration (B-JJ3 eval) covers the behavioral side at the skill level, but there is no shell-level integration test that exercises the full lifecycle. A regression where worktree-create.sh uses a different workspace naming convention than worktree-remove.sh expects would not be caught by the current isolated tests. Suggested fix: add a bats test @test 'jj path: create-then-remove lifecycle succeeds' that exercises both scripts sequentially with a shared mock jj that logs workspace add and forget calls, verifying the name passed to 'jj workspace forget' matches the name used in 'jj workspace add --name'. Location: .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_create.bats or a new test_worktree_lifecycle.bats.

title: worktree-remove.sh: WORKSPACE_NAME unsanitized in jj workspace forget warning message
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh line 105, the warning message embeds WORKSPACE_NAME directly without passing it through sanitize_for_output:
  echo "WARNING: jj workspace forget failed for worktree-${WORKSPACE_NAME}: ..."

This is inconsistent with the validate_safe_name warning at line 46 (which correctly uses sanitize_for_output for the name) and with the jj_err variable on the same line (which does use sanitize_for_output). If WORKSPACE_NAME contains control characters or ANSI escape sequences, the warning output could contain unsanitized content.

The jj command invocation at line 104 is safe (WORKSPACE_NAME is a quoted argument, not interpolated into unquoted shell), but the warning message at line 105 is not.

File: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh, line 105

Suggested fix: Wrap WORKSPACE_NAME with sanitize_for_output in the warning message:
  echo "WARNING: jj workspace forget failed for worktree-$(sanitize_for_output "$WORKSPACE_NAME"): ..."

title: jj eval schema field 'skill' missing from behavioral evals in jj/evals/evals.json
description: The evals schema (pr-review/evals/evals.schema.json) defines a 'skill' field for behavioral evals (described as 'Skill under test for behavioral evals'). In pr-review/evals/evals.json, all behavioral evals correctly include a 'skill' field (e.g., 'skill': 'review-pr'). However, in jj/evals/evals.json, the behavioral evals JJ-B1 through JJ-B10 are all missing the 'skill' field — only JJ-B1 implicitly targets 'jujutsu'. The schema does not list 'skill' in 'required', so this doesn't cause validation errors, but it means behavioral evals can't be filtered by skill, reducing tooling utility. JJ-B8 ('references the VCS detection preamble') is especially notable: it is listed as a behavioral eval but the 'skill' field is absent, making it unclear whether it targets the jujutsu skill or an agent. Suggested fix: add 'skill': 'jujutsu' to JJ-B1 through JJ-B10 in jj/evals/evals.json, matching the pattern in pr-review/evals/evals.json. Location: jj/evals/evals.json lines 80-256.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: finding is stale — all 10 behavioral evals in jj/evals/evals.json already have "skill": "jujutsu" at HEAD 1b73ced.

title: worktree-create.sh: git worktree remove failure in cleanup_on_error is silenced without warning
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh cleanup_on_error(), line 37 uses:
  git worktree remove --force "$WORKTREE_PATH" 2>/dev/null || true

This silently discards git worktree remove failures with no diagnostic output. The jj path in the same function (lines 33-34) does emit a warning when workspace forget fails. The asymmetry means stale .git/worktrees/ metadata from a failed create operation leaves no trace in logs, making post-failure diagnosis harder.

File: .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, lines 36-37

Suggested fix: Replace 'git worktree remove --force ... 2>/dev/null || true' with a pattern that captures and logs the failure, consistent with the jj branch:
  if \! git_err=$(git worktree remove --force "$WORKTREE_PATH" 2>&1); then
    echo "WARNING: cleanup: git worktree remove failed — run 'git worktree prune' manually if needed" >&2
  fi

title: worktree-remove.sh 'could not determine repo root' branch has no test coverage
description: In worktree-remove.sh lines 65-73, there is an untested branch: when detect_repo_root fails AND the inferred parent directory does NOT end with the _worktrees suffix. In this case the script emits a WARNING, skips path validation and VCS cleanup, and proceeds directly to rm -rf. This is a significant code path because it removes a directory without the path-containment safety check (the EXPECTED_PARENT case guard). The test at line 183 ('exits cleanly when _worktrees parent dir does not exist') uses realpath failure on EXPECTED_PARENT as the exit path, which is different from the no-_worktrees-suffix branch. Suggested fix: add a test that sets up a directory outside any git/jj repo where the worktree path parent is named something like 'random-dir' (not ending in _worktrees), confirms the script exits 0 with a WARNING about 'could not determine repo root', and confirms the directory is removed. Location: .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_remove.bats

description: Fix 7 code findings in worktree-remove.sh and worktree-helpers.sh

title: Fix(batch-C): worktree-create.sh code fixes (.39,.7)

title: Fix(batch-A): jj SKILL.md and jj-git-interop doc fixes (.40,.41,.43,.26,.45,.44)
description: Fix 6 documentation findings in jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md and jj-git-interop.md

title: jj log -r 'all()' comment diverges from jj's own recommended syntax for viewing all revisions
description:
File: jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md, line 125

The comment reads: '# View log with all revisions' with the example 'jj log -r all()'

However, jj's own help text for jj log states:
  'Use jj log -r :: to see all revisions.'

Both all() and :: work in jj 0.39, but :: is the idiom recommended in jj's documentation. Using all() is not wrong but deviates from the canonical example that jj itself documents.

Suggested fix: Change example to 'jj log -r ::' and update the comment accordingly, or add a note that both forms work but :: is the conventional form.

title: jj split workaround calls result a 'sibling' but jj new creates a child commit
description:
File: jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md, lines 175-181

The split workaround heading says 'extract specific files into a new sibling' but the procedure creates a child, not a sibling:

  1. jj new <commit>        -- creates a NEW CHILD of <commit> (not a sibling)
  2. jj squash --from <commit> <path1> <path2>  -- moves files FROM <commit> INTO that child

The resulting topology is: <commit (minus files)> -> <new-child (with files)>

A sibling would share the same parent as <commit>. The procedure produces a child-parent relationship instead.

While the workaround achieves a similar practical outcome to jj split (the content ends up separated into distinct commits), the terminology 'sibling' is technically wrong and could confuse users reasoning about commit graph structure.

Suggested fix: Change 'extract specific files into a new sibling' to 'extract specific files into a new child commit' or more precisely 'move specific files out of a commit into its child'.

title: cleanup_on_error in worktree-create.sh does not deregister git worktree metadata on failure after successful git worktree add
description:
File: .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, lines 29-37 and 69-75

The cleanup_on_error trap (lines 29-37) handles jj workspace forget but has no equivalent for git worktree remove. If git worktree add succeeds (line 71) but the script subsequently exits non-zero (e.g., via set -euo pipefail from an unexpected failure, or an OS signal), the cleanup trap will:
1. rm -rf the worktree directory (line 35)
2. cleanup_empty_parent (line 36)

But it will NOT call 'git worktree remove' or 'git worktree prune', leaving stale metadata in .git/worktrees/. This is inconsistent with the jj path which explicitly calls 'jj workspace forget' during cleanup.

In practice, the lefthook install block (lines 78-81) guards with 'if \!', and no code between lines 71 and 84 should cause an unexpected exit under normal conditions. But the gap exists and creates an asymmetry between jj and git cleanup logic.

Suggested fix: Add a git_worktree_created=false flag, set to true after git worktree add succeeds at line 71, and in cleanup_on_error, call 'git worktree remove --force $WORKTREE_PATH 2>/dev/null || git worktree prune 2>/dev/null' when git_worktree_created=true.

title: design specifies 11 worktree-isolated agents get Environment block but implementation covers 12 (including review-gate)
description: The worktree isolation design doc (section 3, Agent Prompt Changes) specifies that 'all 11 worktree-isolated agents' get the Environment block, with review-gate described as 'read-only, no isolation'. The jj VCS support design (Task 7) says '11 worktree agents' and 'Step 5: Update review-gate references — Change git diff to VCS diff in description and input sections'. The implementation gave review-gate an Environment section too (pr-review/agents/review-gate.md:17-26), though it differs from the standard worktree preamble: it says 'Note: You receive the VCS diff as input — you do NOT need to run VCS commands to obtain it.' The CLAUDE.md memory documents '12 agents: 9 review agents + fix-worker, review-gate, verification-runner'. The design doc counts do not consistently match. This is a minor count discrepancy — review-gate's environment block is appropriate but not described in the design. File: /Volumes/Code/github.com/fzymgc-house/fzymgc-house-skills_worktrees/agent-aa9d1a0f/pr-review/agents/review-gate.md:17-26

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: intentional implementation deviation from original design. Documented alongside design docs.

title: jj plugin has no release-please package entry for plugin-level changes
description:
File: release-please-config.json

The pr-review plugin has both a plugin-level key ('pr-review') and skill-level keys ('pr-review/skills/*') in release-please-config.json. The jj plugin only has a skill-level key ('jj/skills/jujutsu') — there is no 'jj' package entry.

This means changes to jj/plugin.json or jj/ plugin-level files that are not under jj/skills/ will not trigger a release-please release PR. The jj/plugin.json version will only be bumped via the jj/skills/jujutsu extra-files entry — but only when the skill changes.

This is a minor inconsistency: the jj plugin version in marketplace.json is indirectly tied to jujutsu skill releases, not plugin-level changes.

Note: The existing homelab plugin also lacks a plugin-level release-please entry (only skill-level keys exist), so this may be intentional for new plugins. But pr-review does have both levels. If plugin-level releases are desired for jj, add a 'jj' package entry alongside the skill entries.

title: realpath unavailable on macOS <12.3 breaks worktree-remove.sh
description:
File: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh lines 27-34

The new worktree-remove.sh calls 'realpath' which is not part of POSIX and is not available on macOS prior to 12.3 (Monterey). On older macOS systems without GNU coreutils installed, the command is missing and the hook will exit 1 at the realpath call.

The hook scripts target developer workstations (user-machine-run, not CI-only), so macOS compatibility matters. The old script had no realpath dependency.

Severity is suggestion rather than critical since macOS 12.3+ is now widely deployed (released 2022) and this project appears to target modern environments.

Suggested fix: add a fallback using 'python3 -c import os.path; ...' or 'perl -MCwd=realpath' as an alternative, or document the macOS version requirement in CLAUDE.md.

title: non-colocated jj repo fallback in detect_repo_root expands scope beyond design out-of-scope statement
description: The jj VCS support design doc explicitly lists 'Non-colocated jj repos (only colocated .jj/ + .git/ supported)' under 'Out of Scope'. However, worktree-helpers.sh detect_repo_root() includes a jj root fallback for non-colocated repos (lines 43-50). The design doc acknowledges this in implementation note 'dr0.13': the implementation expanded scope as a safety net but 'all VCS operations still assume colocated layout'. This creates a partial, undocumented scope expansion. The fallback prevents the hook from failing in non-colocated repos, but does not add real non-colocated support. The out-of-scope statement in the design doc was not updated to reflect the partial change. This is minor — the note documents it — but the design doc's out-of-scope section remains technically inaccurate. File: /Volumes/Code/github.com/fzymgc-house/fzymgc-house-skills_worktrees/agent-aa9d1a0f/.claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh:43-50

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: intentional implementation deviation from original design. Documented alongside design docs.

title: worktree-remove.sh validate_safe_name blocks removal of worktrees with non-conforming basenames
description:
File: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh lines 37-38

New contract restriction: worktree-remove.sh now calls validate_safe_name on WORKSPACE_NAME (the basename of the canonicalized worktree path). This rejects any basename containing characters outside [a-zA-Z0-9_.-] with exit 1, leaving the worktree directory on disk.

If the Claude Code framework ever generates a worktree name containing characters outside this set (e.g., @, +, :, uppercase in a different context), or if a user manually creates a worktree with such a name, WorktreeRemove will silently fail to clean up the directory. There is no way to remove such a worktree through the hook — the user must do it manually.

Currently known framework-generated names follow the pattern 'agent-<hex>' which passes validation. However, the validate_safe_name constraint is not documented in the WorktreeCreate/WorktreeRemove hook contract, so a future change to name generation could silently break cleanup.

Suggested fix: document the name constraint in comments on both hooks, or downgrade to a WARNING (logging the issue but still proceeding with rm -rf) when the basename fails validation.

title: design spec jj cherry-pick equivalent uses 'jj rebase -r' but vcs-equivalence.md matches design correctly
description: Verified: the vcs-equivalence.md correctly implements the design's cherry-pick equivalent as 'jj rebase -r <change-id> -d <target>' (matching design doc section 2 table). The address-findings Phase 4b jj integration flow also correctly uses 'jj rebase -r <change-id> -d <pr-bookmark>'. No deviation found here — this is a confirmation of alignment. Closing as no action needed. Created in error — should be closed.

title: cleanup_on_error trap fires before jj workspace add in worktree-create.sh when jj not installed or too old
description:
File: .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, lines 29-38

The cleanup_on_error trap is set (line 38) before jj workspace add is called. When jj is not installed (line 47-49) or jj is too old (line 60-62), the early exit 1 fires the trap, which then calls:

  (cd "$REPO_ROOT" && jj workspace forget "worktree-${NAME}" 2>/dev/null)

This calls jj workspace forget for a workspace that was never created. The forget call is harmlessly suppressed (2>/dev/null) but it calls jj — which was just determined to not be installed (line 47) or fails (old version). The 2>/dev/null on the forget call masks the error, so there is no visible problem, but there is a logical inconsistency: attempting to forget a workspace that was never registered.

The secondary concern: if WORKTREE_PARENT was created by mkdir -p (line 40-43) and then an early exit fires (e.g., jj not installed), cleanup_on_error tries to remove WORKTREE_PATH (which doesn't exist — no-op) then calls cleanup_empty_parent on WORKTREE_PARENT. The WORKTREE_PARENT that was just created will be cleaned up if empty. This is actually correct behavior for the mkdir case, but not for the jj-not-installed case where mkdir already succeeded before the check. So the parent directory created by mkdir at line 40-43 is properly cleaned up.

The most actionable fix: Add a flag variable (workspace_created=false) set to true after jj workspace add succeeds, and check it in cleanup_on_error before calling jj workspace forget. This makes the intent explicit and removes the unnecessary jj invocation during cleanup.

title: Inline JSON path escaping in worktree-remove.sh could use existing sanitize_for_output
description: worktree-remove.sh lines 19-22: the early-exit path for a missing directory constructs a JSON warning with inline sed escaping: 'sed "s/[\"]\\\\&/g"'. This one-off JSON-escaping logic is harder to read and differs from how other warnings are formatted (plain text on stderr). Since the JSON output is going to stderr and the key is a static string 'warning', the only variable part is the path — which sanitize_for_output (from worktree-helpers.sh, already sourced) handles for control characters. Using a simple printf with sanitize_for_output would be consistent with the rest of the file and eliminate the cryptic sed pattern. Alternatively, emitting this warning as plain text (matching all other warnings in the file) would remove the one-off JSON format entirely.

title: jujutsu SKILL.md uses granular allowed-tools list instead of wildcard Bash(jj *) from plan
description: The jj VCS support plan (Task 3) specified the SKILL.md frontmatter should have a single allowed-tools entry: '- "Bash(jj *)"'. The implementation instead uses a granular list of 15+ specific jj subcommand patterns (jj log *, jj diff *, jj st *, jj show *, jj bookmark *, jj workspace *, jj git *, jj new *, jj commit *, jj squash *, jj restore *, jj describe *, jj abandon *, jj rebase *, test *). This is a least-privilege improvement over the wildcard approach but deviates from the plan's specification. The granular list is more secure and follows best practices. However, jj absorb (documented in the SKILL.md body as a supported command) has no corresponding allowed-tools entry — agents may be blocked from running 'jj absorb'. Files: /Volumes/Code/github.com/fzymgc-house/fzymgc-house-skills_worktrees/agent-aa9d1a0f/jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md:1-26

title: CI drift-check: validation regex allows '..' in release-please-config keys, permitting path traversal in find
description:
File: .github/workflows/check-skills.yml, line 29.

The drift-check job validates release-please-config.json keys with:
  if [[ "$key" =~ [^a-zA-Z0-9_./-] ]]; then exit 1; fi

The character set allows '.' and '/', meaning '../../../etc' passes validation (each character is a '.', '/', or alphanumeric). This key is then used in:
  find "$key/skills" -name SKILL.md -exec dirname {} \; 2>/dev/null

A PR that adds '../../sensitive' as a package key in release-please-config.json would cause find to traverse outside the checkout directory on the CI runner. The output goes to the 'configured' variable and is diffed — no exec of found paths — so impact is limited to information disclosure (file existence) on the Actions runner filesystem.

This requires a malicious PR author and would be visible in code review. The risk is low but the fix is simple: add a check that keys cannot begin with '..' or contain '..':
  if [[ "$key" == ..* || "$key" == */../* || "$key" == *'/.."' ]]; then exit 1; fi

Or use a stricter positive allowlist:
  if [[ \! "$key" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_./-]*$ ]]; then exit 1; fi

title: CI drift-check iterates all_keys twice: once for validation, once for expansion
description: .github/workflows/check-skills.yml lines 28-39: the drift-check script iterates all_keys twice. First in a while-read loop (lines 28-33) to validate characters and exit 1 on unsafe input. Then again in a subshell (lines 34-39) to expand keys to skill directories. These two passes could be merged into one: perform the character check inside the subshell, but use '|| { echo error; exit 1; }' with 'set -e' or emit a sentinel value on invalid keys and check for it after. As written, the two-pass approach is readable and the script is short, but the duplication of the read loop and the comment explaining the subshell split (line 26-27) suggest the structure is not obvious. A single-pass approach would be cleaner. Priority is suggestion-level since the script is short and correctness is not affected.

This is a sound architectural improvement — single source of truth instead of duplicating the detection code 15+ times — but it represents a design change that happened during implementation. The actual preamble content matches what the design doc specified. title: jj eval JJ-B10 missing 'skill' field; JJ-T5 has ambiguous context for negative triggering
description: Two structural issues in jj/evals/evals.json: (1) JJ-B10 (lines 240-253) is typed 'behavioral' but lacks a 'skill' field that all other behavioral evals include (e.g., JJ-B1 has 'skill': 'jujutsu'). Without the skill field, eval runners may not know which skill to activate. (2) JJ-T5 (lines 39-44) is a negative triggering eval ('commit this with a message should NOT trigger jujutsu unless .jj/ is present') but has no 'context' field, leaving the execution environment ambiguous. The expected_output comment acknowledges the condition, but a formal context field (e.g., 'No .jj/ directory present') is needed for eval tooling to set up the right environment. title: Duplicated _mock_workspace_add function body in create_logging_jj_mock
description: .claude/hooks/tests/helpers.bash: create_logging_jj_mock (lines 87-124) contains an inline _mock_workspace_add function (lines 89-102) that is verbatim identical to the one generated by _write_mock_jj (lines 18-31). Because create_logging_jj_mock needs to log args before dispatching, it cannot call _write_mock_jj directly, but the helper function body is pure duplication. Extract _mock_workspace_add as a shared shell snippet string variable and interpolate it into both heredocs. Example: WORKSPACE_ADD_FN='_mock_workspace_add() { ... }' then use ${WORKSPACE_ADD_FN} in both templates. This eliminates the copy-paste and ensures both mocks stay in sync if the mock behavior ever changes.

title: Redundant cd-to-REPO_ROOT guard in worktree-remove.sh jj cleanup block
description: worktree-remove.sh lines 67-73: the jj cleanup block has a dead-code guard 'elif \! (cd "$REPO_ROOT" 2>/dev/null)'. This check is unreachable in practice: REPO_ROOT was set by detect_repo_root (line 43) which already validates the directory exists before returning. If detect_repo_root succeeds, cd REPO_ROOT cannot fail. The extra elif branch adds visual noise and complexity without any protective value. Simplify to two branches: (1) if jj not installed, warn; (2) elif jj workspace forget fails, warn. Remove the intermediate cd check entirely.

title: CI bats job installs system bats without version pin
description: The 'bats' CI job in .github/workflows/check-skills.yml installs bats via 'apt-get install -y bats' without specifying a version. The Ubuntu LTS apt package for bats lags behind upstream releases; if Ubuntu bumps or changes the packaged version, tests relying on specific bats behavior (e.g., `run` semantics, `load` path resolution) could silently break or start erroring differently. Recommended fix: pin to bats-core via a GitHub release download (similar to how rumdl is installed in the lint job) or use the bats-core/bats-action GitHub Action with a pinned version. title: sanitize_for_output unit test exercises tr directly, not the function itself
description: test_worktree_helpers.bats line 26-31 (the 'strips null bytes' test) does not call sanitize_for_output at all — it reimplements the tr invocation inline. If sanitize_for_output's implementation changes (e.g., the tr range changes), this test would not catch the regression. All other sanitize_for_output tests correctly call the function via source. The null-bytes test should be rewritten to call sanitize_for_output and use an approach compatible with bash's command-substitution null-stripping (e.g., write output to a temp file before asserting). File: /Volumes/Code/github.com/fzymgc-house/fzymgc-house-skills/.claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_helpers.bats:26-31

title: trap - EXIT cleared before echo on stdout — broken pipe leaves worktree orphaned without cleanup
description: worktree-create.sh lines 84-85: 'trap - EXIT' disarms the cleanup handler before 'echo "$WORKTREE_PATH"'. If the echo fails (broken pipe when the framework closes stdin early, or SIGPIPE), the exit trap is already cleared, so cleanup_on_error never runs and the newly created worktree/workspace is left orphaned. The framework may then not know the path was created. While broken-pipe on echo is uncommon, it is possible in any pipeline-based hook system. Suggested fix: move 'trap - EXIT' to after the echo, or capture the echo exit code and conditionally run cleanup: echo "$WORKTREE_PATH" || { cleanup_on_error; exit 1; }; trap - EXIT.

title: worktree-remove.sh 'cannot cd to repo root' branch is untested

This path exists to guard against jj workspace forget being attempted on an inaccessible repo root. There is no BATS test that exercises this path. Suggested test: create a jj repo, detect its root, then chmod 000 the repo root directory, then call worktree-remove.sh — expect exit 0 with 'WARNING' and 'cannot cd to repo root' in output. title: validate_safe_name and cleanup_empty_parent have no direct unit tests in test_worktree_helpers.bats

Both functions contain meaningful logic (regex validation with dot/dotdot/trailing-dot rules; rmdir-on-empty with warning on failure) that can break independently of the create/remove integration paths. Suggested additions: (1) validate_safe_name with empty name, leading-dot, trailing-dot, dotdot-component, space, shell metachar, and valid-name inputs; (2) cleanup_empty_parent with non-existent dir, empty dir (rmdir should fire), and non-empty dir (rmdir should NOT fire). title: Redundant and misleading cd pre-check in jj workspace forget path masks the real error source
description: worktree-remove.sh lines 69-71: the elif branch checks 'elif \! (cd "$REPO_ROOT" 2>/dev/null)' in a subshell to guard against cd failure, but then line 71 runs 'cd "$REPO_ROOT" && jj workspace forget ...' anyway. The subshell test does not prevent the real cd+jj compound from running if the subshell check passes — it only checks that cd can execute, not that jj can run. More importantly, the subshell cd check runs with set -euo pipefail NOT inherited from the parent (subshells in bash inherit pipefail but not errexit by default; they do inherit errexit — however the 2>/dev/null suppresses the error output, making any cd failure silent within the subshell). The check is largely redundant since the actual command on line 71 also does 'cd "$REPO_ROOT" &&' which would surface a failure. The pre-check adds code complexity without adding safety. Suggested fix: remove the redundant elif branch; the 'cd "$REPO_ROOT" && jj workspace forget' on line 71 already handles cd failure (the && short-circuits). If cd fails there, the jj_err capture will contain the failure message which is surfaced in the WARNING.
