title: VCS discriminated-union rules duplicated across fix-worker and verification-runner
description:
The VCS discriminated-union rules (which fields are required/forbidden per VCS) are copy-pasted identically in two agent files:

pr-review/agents/fix-worker.md lines 99-103:
  - When VCS: git — WORKTREE_BRANCH MUST be present, CHANGE_ID MUST be omitted
  - When VCS: jj — CHANGE_ID MUST be present, WORKTREE_BRANCH MUST be omitted
  - Omit means do not include the line at all (not an empty value)

pr-review/agents/verification-runner.md lines 155-158:
  - When VCS: git: MUST include WORKTREE_BRANCH, MUST NOT include CHANGE_ID
  - When VCS: jj: MUST include CHANGE_ID, MUST NOT include WORKTREE_BRANCH

These are functionally identical rules stated twice. If the protocol changes, both files need to be updated in sync.

The rules could be defined once in pr-review/references/vcs-equivalence.md under an 'Agent Output Format' section (which already partially exists at lines 43-52), and both agents can reference that section with 'Follow VCS output rules in vcs-equivalence.md'.

This is a maintainability issue — low urgency but increases drift risk.

Files: pr-review/agents/fix-worker.md lines 99-103, pr-review/agents/verification-runner.md lines 155-158, pr-review/references/vcs-equivalence.md

title: vcs-equivalence.md description incorrectly limits scope to colocated jj repos
description:
pr-review/references/vcs-equivalence.md opens with: 'Use this reference when the repo has .jj/ (colocated jj repo).' However, the worktree hooks (worktree-helpers.sh detect_repo_root) and the vcs-detection-preamble.md both explicitly support non-colocated jj repos (pure jj, no .git/). The equivalence table applies equally to non-colocated repos.

The parenthetical '(colocated jj repo)' misleads agents into thinking the reference is irrelevant for non-colocated repos.

Fix: change line 4 from:
  'Use this reference when the repo has .jj/ (colocated jj repo).'
to:
  'Use this reference when the repo has .jj/ (colocated or standalone jj repo).'

Or simply remove the parenthetical: 'Use this reference when the repo has .jj/.'

File: pr-review/references/vcs-equivalence.md, line 4

title: Redundant cd-test before jj workspace add in worktree-create.sh
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh lines 66-70, there is a standalone cd-accessibility test that checks whether the script can cd to REPO_ROOT before running jj:

  if ! (cd "$REPO_ROOT" 2>/dev/null); then
    echo "ERROR: cannot cd to repo root ..." >&2
    cleanup_on_error
    exit 1
  fi
  if ! jj_out=$(cd "$REPO_ROOT" && jj workspace add ...); then

The separate cd test is redundant because the immediately following command (line 71-72) performs the same cd in the same subshell. If cd fails in line 71, the && short-circuits and jj is not run, and the error is caught by the outer 'if !' block. The distinct error message for 'cannot cd to repo root' is the only marginal value here.

The check at lines 66-70 can be removed without loss of correctness. The jj workspace add error message (line 73) would absorb both cd failures and jj failures, which is acceptable since cd failures to a just-validated repo root are extremely rare.

File: .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, lines 66-70

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: duplicate of dr0.5 — both describe the same redundant subshell cd test in worktree-create.sh lines 66-70.

title: worktree-remove.sh new path validation may reject worktrees created outside _worktrees sibling
description:
The updated .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh adds a new validation step that checks whether WORKTREE_PATH is inside the expected sibling _worktrees/ directory. If the path is outside, the script exits 1 with an error rather than proceeding with cleanup.

Location: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh, lines 1284-1290 in the diff:
  case "$WORKTREE_PATH" in
    "$EXPECTED_PARENT"/*)  ;;  # safe
    *)
      echo ERROR: WORKTREE_PATH is outside expected parent
      exit 1

This is an additive safety guard. It would only be breaking if callers ever invoke worktree-remove.sh with paths outside the expected sibling layout (e.g., worktrees in .claude/worktrees/ from the old layout, or manually created paths). However, CLAUDE.md states the sibling layout has been the established convention, so this should be backward-compatible for standard usage.

The old behavior silently exited 0 when the path didn't exist (line 1234-1237 in old code) vs now properly validating the expected parent. Any automation that relied on worktree-remove.sh being permissive about arbitrary paths will break.

This is classified as suggestion because the sibling layout is documented as the only supported layout and no code in the repo appears to create worktrees in other locations.

title: CLAUDE.md VCS-specific behavior comment understates jj integration flow
description:
CLAUDE.md lines 183-184 state:

  - git: fix-worker reports WORKTREE_BRANCH; orchestrator uses cherry-pick to integrate
  - jj: fix-worker reports CHANGE_ID; orchestrator uses 'jj rebase' to integrate (no cherry-pick)

The jj description is incomplete and partially misleading. The orchestrator flow in jj repos involves more than just 'jj rebase': it also needs 'jj bookmark set <pr-bookmark> -r <change-id>' after rebase to advance the bookmark, and 'jj workspace forget <name>' to clean up. Saying only 'jj rebase' omits these required steps and may lead readers to believe the integration is simpler than it is.

Also, the statement 'orchestrator uses cherry-pick to integrate' for git is accurate, but the jj equivalent is rebase (-r single revision), which is conceptually a cherry-pick but the wording 'jj rebase' without qualification may confuse readers about whether a branch rebase or single-change rebase is meant.

Suggested fix: Expand the jj bullet to clarify the full flow:
  - jj: fix-worker reports CHANGE_ID; orchestrator uses 'jj rebase -r <change-id> -d <pr-bookmark>' to integrate, then advances the bookmark and forgets the workspace

File: /CLAUDE.md lines 183-184

title: worktree-create.sh lefthook install runs for jj repos, deviating from spec code sample
description: The design spec (docs/plans/2026-03-07-jj-vcs-support-design.md, worktree-create.sh section) shows lefthook install only in the git branch of the conditional: 'if lefthook.yml exists { lefthook install }' inside the else (git) branch. Similarly the implementation plan (Task 8, Step 2) shows lefthook install only in the git branch. The actual implementation (.claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, lines 86-91) moves lefthook install outside the VCS conditional, running it for BOTH jj and git repos. The comment says 'lefthook works in both VCS modes'. This is a sensible improvement but deviates from the documented code sample. Since lefthook manages git hooks which depend on .git/, running lefthook install in a non-colocated jj workspace would fail — though for colocated repos (which have .git/) it works fine. No finding if only colocated repos are supported, but worth documenting the intentional deviation.

title: sanitize_for_output comment omits DEL (0x7F) from list of stripped characters
description:
The sanitize_for_output function comment in .claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh line 5-6 reads:

  # Strip C0 control chars (0x00-0x1F except newline/tab) and C1 control chars (0x80-0x9F)

But the implementation also strips \177 (0x7F = DEL, the delete character), which is not mentioned in the comment. DEL is not a C0 or C1 control character -- it sits between the printable ASCII range and the extended range.

The tr command is:
  tr -d '\000-\010\013-\037\177\200-\237'

The \177 range element strips DEL (0x7F) but the comment doesn't mention this.

Suggested fix: Update the comment to mention DEL:
  # Strip C0 control chars (0x00-0x1F except newline/tab), DEL (0x7F), and C1 control chars (0x80-0x9F)

File: /.claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh line 5

title: Plan docs for prior PR review iterations committed to main branch (YAGNI)
description: The PR commits three plan docs specifically for fixing findings on THIS PR's review: docs/plans/2026-03-07-pr31-review-findings-fix.md, docs/plans/2026-03-07-pr31-review-fixes.md, and docs/plans/2026-03-08-pr31-turn3-review-fixes.md. These are ephemeral scaffolding documents for the PR review iteration process — they describe how to fix review comments on this PR itself. Once merged, these are effectively dead documents with no future utility. They inflate the docs/plans/ directory with meta-review artifacts that are irrelevant after merge. Consider omitting these from the PR or storing them in a separate location (e.g., a gitignored directory or PR description). The design doc (2026-03-07-jj-vcs-support-design.md) and implementation plan (2026-03-07-jj-vcs-support.md) are genuinely useful future reference and are appropriate to commit.

title: release-please-config uses jj/skills/jujutsu key instead of jj as specified in implementation plan
description: The implementation plan (docs/plans/2026-03-07-jj-vcs-support.md, Task 9, Step 1) specifies: 'Add jj entry to packages' and Step 2: 'Add "jj": "0.1.0" to .release-please-manifest.json'. The actual implementation uses 'jj/skills/jujutsu' as the package key in both release-please-config.json (line 86) and .release-please-manifest.json (line 10). The implementation is consistent with the existing pattern (e.g., homelab/skills/grafana, pr-review/skills/review-pr) so it is arguably more correct. However, the implementation plan and manifest manifest are inconsistent with each other. The plan should be updated to reflect the actual key used, or the deviation documented.

title: jujutsu SKILL.md uses broad Bash wildcard while address-findings uses per-command restrictions
description:
The allowed-tools type in SKILL.md YAML frontmatter is inconsistently constrained across the new jj plugin vs the pr-review plugin:

jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md:
  allowed-tools:
    - 'Bash(jj *)'      # permits any jj subcommand
    - 'Bash(test *)'

pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md (new entries in this PR):
  allowed-tools:
    - 'Bash(jj st *)'
    - 'Bash(jj log *)'
    - 'Bash(jj diff *)'
    - 'Bash(jj workspace *)'
    - 'Bash(jj rebase *)'
    - 'Bash(jj bookmark *)'
    - 'Bash(jj git *)'
    - 'Bash(jj undo *)'
    - 'Bash(jj describe *)'
    - 'Bash(jj new *)'
    - 'Bash(jj commit *)'

The jujutsu skill is specifically a jj workflow guidance skill -- 'Bash(jj *)' is appropriate since the skill's entire purpose is running arbitrary jj commands. However, this inconsistency means a reviewer cannot tell whether 'Bash(jj *)' is intentional (jj-purpose skill) or accidental (forgot to restrict). Document the design intent with a comment or align to a consistent pattern (broad for purpose-specific skills, narrow for orchestrators).

title: Non-colocated jj repo support implemented beyond spec scope
description: Design doc (docs/plans/2026-03-07-jj-vcs-support-design.md, Out of Scope section) explicitly excludes 'Non-colocated jj repos (only colocated .jj/ + .git/ supported)'. However the implementation adds jj root fallback for non-colocated repos in .claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh (lines 43-50) and BATS tests cover it (.claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_create.bats lines 75, 226). No design amendment documents this scope extension. Suggestion: Update the design doc to reflect the expanded scope, or add a comment explaining why the jj root fallback was added as a safety net beyond the stated scope.

title: fix-worker discriminated union output contract expressed only in prose
description:
The fix-worker output format is a VCS-discriminated union: when VCS is 'git', WORKTREE_BRANCH must be present and CHANGE_ID must be absent; when VCS is 'jj', CHANGE_ID must be present and WORKTREE_BRANCH must be absent. This contract is documented in prose in three places (fix-worker.md, verification-runner.md, address-findings/SKILL.md) but has no machine-readable schema.

The discriminated union rules in fix-worker.md:
- 'When VCS: git -- WORKTREE_BRANCH MUST be present, CHANGE_ID MUST be omitted'
- 'When VCS: jj -- CHANGE_ID MUST be present, WORKTREE_BRANCH MUST be omitted'

The orchestrator's Phase 4 collect step must parse this correctly or misroute the integration method. A fix-worker that emits both fields or neither field would not be caught by any structural validation.

Suggested fix: Define the output schema as a single reference block in a shared reference file (e.g., pr-review/references/fix-worker-output.md) with the discriminated union clearly stated. The prose in fix-worker.md and address-findings already does this reasonably -- the suggestion is to extract it to one canonical location rather than three. The existing discriminant rules in fix-worker.md are well-specified; this is a DRY concern.

title: evals.json assertion type field is an undocumented open string enum
description:
Both jj/evals/evals.json and pr-review/evals/evals.json use an assertion 'type' field that is an implicit string enum with values: skill_triggered, skill_not_triggered, command_invoked, contains, manual. This invariant is enforced only by convention -- there is no schema, no documented enum, and no validation. A typo like 'skill_trigger' or 'triggered' would silently produce a no-op eval.

File locations:
- jj/evals/evals.json (new file, all 15 evals)
- pr-review/evals/evals.json (modified, assertions added to T1-T10)

Suggested fix: Add a JSON Schema file (e.g., evals.schema.json) that enumerates valid assertion types, and reference it via the schema field in evals.json. Alternatively, document the enum values in a comment block or README adjacent to the evals files. At minimum, add a top-level assertion_types field to the schema documenting the valid set.

title: sanitize_for_output helper has no dedicated unit tests
description:
worktree-helpers.sh exports sanitize_for_output() which strips C0/C1 control characters from strings used in error messages. This function is security-relevant: it prevents ANSI escape injection or terminal control sequences from being written to hook output that Claude reads and displays.

No test in test_worktree_create.bats or test_worktree_remove.bats directly exercises sanitize_for_output() with control character inputs to verify the stripping behavior. Existing tests only verify the sanitized output of other paths (error messages) where the input is already clean.

A test passing a name or error message containing ANSI codes, null bytes, or BEL characters to the hook and verifying the output is clean would prevent regressions if the tr pattern is accidentally changed.

File: .claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh:4-8, .claude/hooks/tests/ (no test file)

title: cleanup_on_error jj workspace forget not tested in worktree-create
description:
worktree-create.sh cleanup_on_error() calls 'jj workspace forget' to clean up orphaned workspace metadata when jj workspace add fails after metadata was already created (.claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh lines 29-36). The existing test 'jj path: cleans up worktree and parent on workspace add failure' (test_worktree_create.bats:140) verifies the directory is removed but uses create_failing_jj_mock which does NOT handle the workspace forget call (the mock exits 1 for all non-help invocations).

This means the test doesn't verify that jj workspace forget is actually invoked during cleanup — it only verifies the directory is absent. If the cleanup_on_error forget logic were accidentally deleted or broken, the test would still pass.

A complementary test using create_logging_jj_mock to capture the workspace forget invocation during failure cleanup would close this gap.

File: .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_create.bats, .claude/hooks/tests/helpers.bash

title: redundant subshell cd test before jj workspace add — dead guard code
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh lines 66-70, the code checks whether cd to $REPO_ROOT works via a subshell test:

  if ! (cd "$REPO_ROOT" 2>/dev/null); then
    echo "ERROR: cannot cd to repo root..." >&2
    cleanup_on_error
    exit 1
  fi

This check is entirely redundant. The actual workspace add command immediately follows on line 71:

  if ! jj_out=$(cd "$REPO_ROOT" && jj workspace add ... 2>&1); then

If 'cd "$REPO_ROOT"' fails on line 71, the '&&' short-circuits and jj workspace add does not run, causing the entire command substitution to return non-zero — which is caught by the 'if !' guard on line 71. The error on line 71 would produce a confusing message ('jj workspace add failed') when the actual cause is a cd failure, but this is better addressed by splitting the cd and jj invocations rather than adding a pre-check that doesn't prevent the same cd from running again.

The dead guard adds complexity without adding safety. If the intent is a clearer error message for cd failure specifically, the fix is to separate 'cd "$REPO_ROOT"' into its own error-handled step rather than a separate subshell test.

File: .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, lines 66-70.

title: jj workspace forget failure silently suppressed in cleanup_on_error
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh lines 31-33, the cleanup_on_error function calls 'jj workspace forget ... 2>/dev/null || true', which suppresses all output and ignores all exit codes. If jj workspace forget fails (e.g., workspace was never created, jj has a bug, permissions issue), orphaned jj workspace metadata will remain in the repository with no indication to the user.

The pattern is intentional for cleanup contexts (best-effort cleanup), but a warning message would help diagnose lingering jj workspace entries that appear in 'jj workspace list' after failed worktree creation.

Suggested fix: Change the line to:
  (cd "$REPO_ROOT" && jj workspace forget "worktree-${NAME}" 2>/dev/null) ||     echo "WARNING: cleanup: jj workspace forget worktree-${NAME} failed — run manually if needed" >&2

This keeps cleanup non-fatal while surfacing actionable information.

File: .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, lines 31-33.

title: sanitize_for_output preserves newlines, breaking JSON stderr output in worktree-remove.sh
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh lines 1241-1244, a warning is emitted as a JSON string to stderr when the worktree directory is already gone. The sanitize_for_output function (worktree-helpers.sh) strips most control characters but its tr range (\000-\010, \013-\037) intentionally excludes 0x0A (newline). A WORKTREE_PATH containing a newline (possible from malformed jq -r output) produces broken JSON to stderr.

Impact is limited to stderr only — no command injection, no downstream parsing of this JSON. However if a consumer ever parses this JSON stream, it would fail on multi-line values.

Suggested fix: extend sanitize_for_output to also strip newlines and tabs:
  printf '%s' "$input" | LC_ALL=C tr -d '\000-\037\177\200-\237'
Or use jq -n --arg msg ... to produce properly escaped JSON.

Files: .claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh (sanitize_for_output), .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh (~line 1243)

title: VCS startup verification method diverged from implementation plan spec (jj workspace root -> path pattern)
description: The implementation plan (docs/plans/2026-03-07-jj-vcs-support.md, Task 7, Step 1) specifies the jj startup verification commands as: 'jj workspace root, jj log -r @'. But the final implementation (pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md) instead uses a shell path-pattern match: 'case $(pwd) in *_worktrees/*) ;;'. The commit (ka4.58) that made this change has a documented technical reason: 'jj 0.39+ does not emit a (current) marker' so jj workspace list cannot reliably identify the current workspace. The approach is sound but represents an undocumented divergence from the plan doc. The vcs-detection-preamble.md does include a 'Do NOT rely on jj workspace list output' note but does not explain the version compatibility issue. Suggested fix: add a brief comment in vcs-detection-preamble.md citing the jj 0.39+ limitation to make this a documented rather than silent deviation from the plan.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: intentional deviation. Path-pattern matching replaced jj workspace list because jj 0.39+ dropped the (current) marker. Documented in commit ka4.58.

title: Non-colocated jj repo support in hooks contradicts design 'Out of Scope' constraint
description: The design doc (docs/plans/2026-03-07-jj-vcs-support-design.md, Out of Scope section) explicitly states: 'Non-colocated jj repos (only colocated .jj/ + .git/ supported)'. However, the implementation added jj root fallback support in detect_repo_root() in .claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh, plus a dedicated BATS test (test_worktree_create.bats line 226: 'pure jj repo (no .git) uses jj root fallback'). The worktree-create.sh and worktree-remove.sh now functionally support pure jj repos (no .git/). The fix plan (2026-03-07-pr31-review-findings-fix.md, xpy.41) originally intended only to add a comment saying non-colocated repos were out of scope and would never reach the code path, but the actual implementation went further by adding real support plus tests. This is a positive capability expansion but contradicts the documented scope. Suggested resolution: update the design doc to reflect the expanded scope, or explicitly document in worktree-helpers.sh that pure jj support is an intentional extension beyond the original spec.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: intentional scope expansion. The jj root fallback in detect_repo_root() was added defensively — it's minimal code that prevents hard failures in pure-jj repos. The cost is negligible and the benefit is robustness.

title: vcs-equivalence.md: git checkout <branch> -> jj new <bookmark> mapping creates new commit rather than switching
description:
File: jj/skills/jujutsu/references/jj-git-interop.md, What NOT To Do table (line 67)

The table maps:
  git checkout <branch>  ->  jj new <bookmark>

This mapping is misleading. 'git checkout <branch>' switches to the tip of an existing branch without creating a new commit. 'jj new <bookmark>' creates a NEW empty commit on top of the bookmark — it does not merely switch to it.

The literal equivalent of 'git checkout <branch>' (switch to edit the tip of a branch) is 'jj edit <bookmark>', not 'jj new <bookmark>'. The jj documentation itself recommends using 'jj new' for most workflows, but the table presents it as a direct equivalent without noting the semantic difference.

A reader who uses 'jj new <bookmark>' when they meant 'git checkout <branch>' will end up with an extra empty commit in their history.

Suggested fix: Add a note to the table row, e.g.:
  | `git checkout <branch>` | `jj new <bookmark>` (creates new child commit; use `jj edit <bookmark>` to edit the tip directly) |

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: stale — addressed by dr0 fixes in current HEAD.

title: jj conflict resolution instructions omit jj-specific conflict marker format
description:
File: jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md, Conflict Handling section (line ~269)

The Resolving Conflicts section says:
  '1. Open the conflicted file and remove conflict markers (`<<<<<<<`, `>>>>>>>`)'

jj uses a different conflict marker format than git. In addition to `<<<<<<<` and `>>>>>>>`, jj conflict markers include `%%%%%%%` (base changes section) and `+++++++` (second parent section). An agent following these instructions will not know to remove the jj-specific `%%%%%%%` and `+++++++` markers, potentially leaving malformed files.

jj conflict marker format:
  <<<<<<< Conflict N of M
  %%%%%%% Changes from base to first parent
  -removed lines
  +added lines
  +++++++ Contents of second parent
  content from second parent
  >>>>>>> Conflict N of M end

Suggested fix: Update the instruction to reference all jj conflict marker types, e.g.:
  '1. Open the conflicted file and remove all conflict markers (`<<<<<<<`, `%%%%%%%`, `+++++++`, `>>>>>>>`)'

title: release-please-config.json uses non-standard 'comment' field with no schema validation
description:
The jj/skills/jujutsu package entry in release-please-config.json adds a 'comment' field that is not part of the release-please package configuration schema:

  'jj/skills/jujutsu': {
    'comment': 'Key uses skill-directory path (not plugin-level jj) for consistency with other skill packages',
    'release-type': 'simple',
    ...
  }

The release-please schema for package entries accepts: release-type, package-name, component, changelog-path, release-as, skip-github-release, draft, prerelease, and extra-files. The 'comment' key is not documented and release-please will silently ignore it. This creates a documentation anti-pattern where the explanation for the non-obvious key choice lives in a field that tooling ignores, rather than in a proper code comment location (e.g., a top-level 'comments' object or a companion README).

File: release-please-config.json, package 'jj/skills/jujutsu'.

Suggested fix: Move the rationale to CLAUDE.md or a release-please section in the repo docs, where it will actually be read. Remove the 'comment' field from the JSON config to avoid confusion about whether it is a valid release-please directive.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: stale — comment field not present in current release-please-config.json.

title: address-findings SKILL.md: jj PR checkout flow pipe to grep returns non-zero exit on no-match
description: In pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md, the jj PR checkout verification step instructs: 'jj bookmark list | grep <pr-branch-name>'. When the bookmark is not found, grep exits with status 1. In an agent shell running under set -e (or if the LLM follows the instruction literally in a script), this would abort the operation prematurely rather than triggering the fallback jj git fetch. The instruction should use 'grep ... || true' or redirect to a variable first, or use 'jj bookmark list | grep -q ... || { jj git fetch; ... }' for explicit error handling. Location: pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md, Phase 1 jj checkout verification block.

title: B-JJ2 and B-JJ3 in pr-review evals missing context field required by behavioral evals
description:
The behavioral evals added in pr-review/evals/evals.json — B-JJ2 and B-JJ3 — are missing the 'context' field that is present on all other behavioral evals (B-JJ1, B-JJ4, B-VCS1 and all pre-existing B1-B6 evals).

B-JJ2 simulates a fix-worker response containing 'VCS: jj' in the prompt itself, and B-JJ3 similarly embeds VCS context in the prompt text. However, the 'context' field serves a different purpose — it describes the environment the eval runner should set up (e.g., repo has .jj/ directory). Without it, an eval runner has no declaration of the prerequisite repo state needed to reproduce the scenario.

This is in contrast to jj/evals/evals.json where JJ-B8 is also missing context, though that eval's prompt is more self-contained ('I'm in a jj repo' is stated explicitly).

File: pr-review/evals/evals.json, evals B-JJ2 and B-JJ3.

Suggested fix: Add 'context' fields to B-JJ2 and B-JJ3, e.g.:
  B-JJ2: 'context': 'Repo root contains .jj/ directory. fix-worker has already committed its changes to a worktree workspace.'
  B-JJ3: 'context': 'Repo root contains .jj/ directory. fix-worker workspace at ../repo_worktrees/fix-abc exists and contains committed changes.'

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: stale — addressed by dr0 fixes in current HEAD.

title: agent Environment section removed: inline path rules deleted from 9 agents, replaced with indirection to reference file
description: All 9 review agents (api-contract-checker, code-reviewer, code-simplifier, comment-analyzer, pr-test-analyzer, security-auditor, silent-failure-hunter, spec-compliance, type-design-analyzer) previously had inline Environment sections specifying: (1) startup verification steps, (2) path rules (relative paths only, no cd outside worktree). These are now replaced with a single line referencing vcs-detection-preamble.md. This is an additive improvement in concision, but it changes the agent interface contract: agents that were previously self-contained (no external file dependency for startup procedure) now depend on pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md being present and accessible. If the plugin is installed without the references/ directory (e.g., selective install or future refactor), all 9 agents lose their startup procedure silently. The path rules (relative paths, no cd outside worktree) are now only in vcs-detection-preamble.md — not in the agent files themselves. Files: pr-review/agents/*.md (all 9 review agents), pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: intentional design. Replacing inline Environment sections with a reference to vcs-detection-preamble.md reduces duplication across 10 agents. The path rules are now in the preamble's Path Rules section.

title: Divergent assertion type vocabulary between jj and pr-review evals schemas
description:
The two evals.json files define assertion type vocabularies that have diverged, with no shared schema or documentation to govern valid values.

jj/evals/evals.json introduces two assertion types not present in pr-review/evals/evals.json:
- 'skill_not_triggered' (used in JJ-T5, JJ-T8)
- 'command_invoked' (used in JJ-T7)

pr-review/evals/evals.json uses 'not_contains' which is absent from jj/evals/evals.json.

Without a shared schema, eval runners must handle an open-ended set of types. The 'skill_not_triggered' / 'not_contains' split is particularly confusing since both express negation but with different semantics, yet the distinction is not documented.

Files:
- jj/evals/evals.json (new file)
- pr-review/evals/evals.json (modified)

Suggested fix: Add a shared reference document or JSON schema defining the valid assertion 'type' values, their semantics, and required fields. Alternatively, normalize 'skill_not_triggered' to match existing vocabulary in pr-review evals.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: stale — addressed by dr0 fixes in current HEAD.

title: release-please-config: jj package-name collides with plugin root key pattern used by other packages
description: The jj skill entry in release-please-config.json uses 'package-name: jj' while the config key is 'jj/skills/jujutsu'. The other skill packages use matching keys and package-names (e.g., 'homelab/skills/grafana' with package-name 'homelab-grafana'). The homelab and pr-review plugins also have separate plugin-level entries. The jj plugin does NOT have a plugin-level entry in release-please-config.json (only the skill-level 'jj/skills/jujutsu' entry), meaning plugin.json version is tracked only via extra-files jsonpath — not via a dedicated package entry. This asymmetry means the jj plugin version (jj/plugin.json) is only bumped when the jujutsu skill changes, not on other jj plugin changes (e.g., adding a new skill or command). Consistent with how homelab and pr-review have both plugin-level and skill-level entries. File: release-please-config.json.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: stale — addressed by dr0 fixes in current HEAD.

title: jj/plugin.json missing skills and commands metadata fields present in other plugin.json files
description: The new jj/plugin.json contains only name, version, and description fields. The marketplace.json entry references it as a plugin source, but the plugin.json for other plugins (homelab, pr-review) include additional fields if any exist. More importantly: the jj plugin includes a slash command (jj/commands/jj-init.md) but the plugin.json does not declare it. If the plugin manifest format requires declaring commands or if the framework uses plugin.json for discovery, this could prevent /jj-init from being found. Compare with how homelab/plugin.json and pr-review/plugin.json declare their resources. If the framework discovers skills/commands by directory scanning this is harmless, but should be verified for consistency. Location: jj/plugin.json.

title: vcs-detection-preamble.md: jj worktree location check relies on fragile path pattern match
description: In pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md, the jj location check uses a shell case pattern match on pwd() output to determine whether the agent is in a worktree workspace: case "$(pwd)" in *_worktrees/*) ;; esac. This is fragile because (a) a user whose repo path happens to contain _worktrees/ in a parent directory would false-positive, and (b) the preamble text also says 'Do NOT rely on jj workspace list output' but does not offer a more robust alternative. A more reliable check would be 'jj workspace root' compared against the main repo root (from jj root), though this has tool-dependency costs. At minimum, the pattern should anchor more tightly (e.g., check that the path ends in _worktrees/<name> or use jj workspace list to compare workspace roots despite the caveat about (current) marker). This is a correctness concern for edge-case repo layouts.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: intentional design. Path-based check is more reliable than jj workspace list (which changed format in 0.39+). False positive on repos inside _worktrees/ parent paths is extremely unlikely in practice.

title: check-skills.yml drift-check: sort vs sort -u inconsistency could mask duplicate entries
description: In .github/workflows/check-skills.yml, the drift-check step uses 'sort' for actual and 'sort -u' for configured. If a skill directory somehow appeared twice in the find output for actual, the diff would spuriously fail even though the config is correct. Both sides should use the same sort flags for a fair comparison. Suggested fix: use 'sort -u' for both, or 'sort' for both. Location: .github/workflows/check-skills.yml, the actual= and configured= assignments.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: stale — sort -u already used consistently in drift-check.

title: sanitize_for_output in worktree-helpers.sh does not strip DEL character (0x7F)
description: In .claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh, the sanitize_for_output function strips C0 control characters (0x00-0x08, 0x0B-0x1F) and C1 characters (0x80-0x9F) but does not include the DEL character (0x7F). DEL is technically a control character that can cause display corruption in terminals and some log parsers. The tr delete range '\000-\010\013-\037\200-\237' stops at 0x1F and does not include 0x7F. While not exploitable in the current context (sanitized output goes to error messages only), the omission is a minor completeness gap. Fix: extend the tr range to include \177 (octal for DEL/0x7F): tr -d '\000-\010\013-\037\177\200-\237'.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: stale — addressed by dr0 fixes in current HEAD.

title: worktree-remove.sh emits invalid JSON to stderr when path contains double quotes
description: In .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh, the warning message at the 'path does not exist' branch (line ~1229 of the diff) constructs JSON via shell string concatenation before path validation has run: echo '{"warning":"worktree directory already removed: '"$(sanitize_for_output "$WORKTREE_PATH")"'"}' >&2. If WORKTREE_PATH (from jq -r '.path // empty') contains a double-quote character (e.g. jq input: {"path": "/tmp/evil\"dir"}), the resulting stderr output is malformed JSON. sanitize_for_output strips control characters but not double quotes or backslashes. Impact is limited to stderr diagnostic output only (the framework reads stdout), so this cannot cause command injection or privilege escalation. However, it could confuse log parsers or monitoring tools that consume stderr JSON. Fix: either use printf '%s' "" | jq -Rs '.' to produce a valid JSON string, or move the warning after validate_safe_name has already rejected paths with problematic characters.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: fixed (duplicate of fpr.21). JSON warning now escapes backslash and double-quote characters via sed.

title: worktree-remove.sh error path repeats WORKSPACE_NAME in warning message construction
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh the jj workspace forget warning message constructs the workspace name twice:

  echo "WARNING: jj workspace forget failed for worktree-${WORKSPACE_NAME}: ... (run 'jj workspace forget worktree-${WORKSPACE_NAME}' manually to clean up)" >&2

The string 'worktree-${WORKSPACE_NAME}' is referenced twice inline. This is minor but could be simplified by assigning it to a local variable:

  JJ_WORKSPACE_NAME="worktree-${WORKSPACE_NAME}"
  if ! jj_err=$(cd "$REPO_ROOT" && jj workspace forget "$JJ_WORKSPACE_NAME" 2>&1); then
    echo "WARNING: jj workspace forget failed for ${JJ_WORKSPACE_NAME}: ... (run 'jj workspace forget ${JJ_WORKSPACE_NAME}' manually)" >&2
  fi

This also makes the variable available for reuse if the forget-name pattern needs to change.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: too minor. The string appears twice in one message — extracting to a variable adds a line for negligible clarity gain.

title: GHA workflow check-skills.yml missing explicit permissions: read-all declaration
description: The .github/workflows/check-skills.yml workflow has no top-level permissions: block. While the pull_request trigger gives read-only tokens by default for fork PRs, explicitly declaring permissions: contents: read is a defense-in-depth hardening measure per GitHub Actions security best practices. The lint job already uses GH_TOKEN for downloading rumdl, which only requires read access. Suggested fix: add 'permissions: contents: read' at the workflow level in .github/workflows/check-skills.yml. This ensures any future job additions do not accidentally inherit broader permissions.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: stale — permissions: contents: read already present in check-skills.yml.

title: VCS discriminant rules for output format duplicated across fix-worker.md and verification-runner.md
description:
Both pr-review/agents/fix-worker.md and pr-review/agents/verification-runner.md define the same VCS discriminant rules for the output format block:

fix-worker.md:
  - When VCS: git -- WORKTREE_BRANCH MUST be present, CHANGE_ID MUST be omitted
  - When VCS: jj -- CHANGE_ID MUST be present, WORKTREE_BRANCH MUST be omitted

verification-runner.md:
  - When VCS: git: MUST include WORKTREE_BRANCH, MUST NOT include CHANGE_ID
  - When VCS: jj: MUST include CHANGE_ID, MUST NOT include WORKTREE_BRANCH

These are the same rule stated twice. verification-runner.md even cross-references fix-worker ('The VCS discriminant fields follow fix-worker's rules') but then restates them anyway.

Suggested fix: In verification-runner.md, remove the restatement and keep only the cross-reference sentence: 'The VCS discriminant fields follow fix-worker's rules (see fix-worker.md Output section).' This is already present but currently followed by a duplicate enumeration.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: intentional. Each agent file should be self-contained with its output contract. Cross-referencing another agent's output format reduces agent autonomy and adds fragile coupling.

title: VCS detection one-liner repeated across address-findings SKILL.md and vcs-detection-preamble.md
description:
The VCS detection shell one-liner appears in two places with slight wording differences:

1. pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md (step 1):
   'if test -d .jj; then echo "jj"; elif test -d .git; then echo "git"; else echo "none"; fi'

2. pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md (Phase 4, step 5 fallback):
   'if test -d .jj; then echo jj; elif test -d .git; then echo git; else echo none; fi'
   (quotes differ: no quotes around jj/git/none)

These are semantically identical but textually inconsistent. The address-findings skill also duplicates the intent of the preamble inline rather than simply referencing it.

Suggested fix: In address-findings SKILL.md Phase 4 step 5, replace the inline detection snippet with a reference: 'Detect VCS using the procedure in pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md' rather than restating the command. This keeps the canonical form in one file.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: not a real issue. 'echo jj' and 'echo "jj"' are semantically identical in shell. The one-liner is two different documents with slightly different style.

title: empty WORKTREE_PATH in worktree-remove.sh exits 0 silently, masking malformed input
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh lines 13-15, when WORKTREE_PATH is empty (e.g., jq returns empty because 'path' field is missing or null in the input JSON), the script exits 0 silently with no message. This masks malformed input from the Claude framework.

While silent exit 0 may be intentional to make WorktreeRemove idempotent, it makes it impossible to distinguish between 'no path provided (framework bug)' and 'path provided but already removed'. The already-removed case now emits a JSON warning (line 18-19), but the missing-path case is completely silent.

Location: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh lines 13-15.

Suggested fix: emit a warning to stderr when WORKTREE_PATH is empty so the framework can surface it, while still exiting 0 to avoid blocking teardown:
  if [[ -z "$WORKTREE_PATH" ]]; then
    echo '{"warning":"no path field in WorktreeRemove input — skipping removal"}' >&2
    exit 0
  fi

title: cleanup_on_error defined inline rather than sourced from worktree-helpers.sh

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: intentional. cleanup_on_error references local variables (REPO_ROOT, NAME, WORKTREE_PATH, WORKTREE_PARENT) — moving to helpers.sh would require passing 4+ arguments for no real benefit.

title: Inline jj mock in test_worktree_create.bats duplicates helpers.bash capability
description:
In .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_create.bats, the 'detect_repo_root falls back to jj root when git rev-parse fails' test (around line 93) builds a full inline jj mock from scratch rather than using the helpers.bash utilities. Similarly, test_worktree_remove.bats has the same pattern for its own detect_repo_root fallback test.

These inline mocks duplicate the workspace-add and root handler logic already present in create_pure_jj_mock/create_mock_jj. Both tests need a 'non-git dir with .jj/' scenario.

Suggested fix: Extend helpers.bash with a create_jj_root_only_mock (or reuse create_pure_jj_mock with the JJ_REPO_ROOT env var), and replace the inline heredocs in both test files with a helper call. This keeps all mock construction in one place and makes the test intention clearer.

title: cleanup_on_error not registered as trap — set -e bypasses cleanup on unexpected exits
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, cleanup_on_error() is defined as a plain function (lines 29-32) and called explicitly after each known failure point. However, with 'set -euo pipefail' active, any unexpected command failure (e.g., jq failing, dirname failing, basename failing) will cause the script to exit immediately without calling cleanup_on_error. This leaves a partial WORKTREE_PARENT directory and possibly a partial WORKTREE_PATH.

Since WORKTREE_PARENT is created at line 34 via 'mkdir -p', any exit after that point without calling cleanup_on_error leaves an empty parent directory on disk (which worktree-remove.sh may not be triggered to clean up, since the framework only calls WorktreeRemove when it has a known worktree path).

Suggested fix: register cleanup_on_error as an EXIT trap after WORKTREE_PARENT is created, with a flag to suppress it on success:
  _CLEANUP_NEEDED=0
  trap '[[ $_CLEANUP_NEEDED -eq 1 ]] && cleanup_on_error' EXIT
  mkdir -p "$WORKTREE_PARENT" || exit 1
  _CLEANUP_NEEDED=1
  ... (on success path)
  _CLEANUP_NEEDED=0  # suppress cleanup
  echo "$WORKTREE_PATH"

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: stale — addressed by dr0 fixes in current HEAD.

title: Duplicate _mock_workspace_add function body across create_mock_jj and create_pure_jj_mock
Missing eval: jj push --change auto-bookmark naming convention
description: jj/evals/evals.json covers triggering (JJ-T1..T8) and behavioral (JJ-B1..B9) scenarios for the jujutsu skill but has no eval for the jj push --change auto-bookmark convention. The CLAUDE.md MEMORY notes this as a documented gotcha (ka4.67): 'jj push --change auto-bookmark naming convention'. Without an eval, a regression in the skill's guidance on this critical workflow (pushing without a pre-set bookmark) would go undetected. Suggested eval JJ-B10: prompt='push changes that have no bookmark set', expected=agent suggests jj git push --change @- or creates a bookmark first. File: jj/evals/evals.json

title: Missing test: worktree-remove detect_repo_root total failure in pure-jj repo
description: test_worktree_remove.bats has 'fails when git rev-parse cannot determine repo root' (line 192) but it exercises a non-.jj directory where jj is simply absent from PATH. The test passes because detect_repo_root's jj fallback silently skips when jj is not installed. However, there is no test for the case where .jj/ IS present AND jj is installed but 'jj root' returns empty or fails — the third branch of detect_repo_root in worktree-helpers.sh. This matters for broken jj installs or jj repos whose workspace is corrupted. Add a test: 'detect_repo_root fails when both git and jj root return empty' using create_always_failing_jj_mock with a .jj directory present, expecting exit 1 with 'could not determine repo root'. File: .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_remove.bats

title: Missing test: jj lefthook-failure warning in worktree-create jj path
description: test_worktree_create.bats has 'warns when lefthook install fails' (line 263) for the git path but no equivalent test for the jj path. The jj lefthook test at line 283 only covers the success case. If lefthook install fails in a jj worktree, the WARNING branch in worktree-create.sh line 80-82 is exercised by the git test but the jj variant is never hit in the test suite. A parallel 'jj path: warns when lefthook install fails' test should be added to verify the same soft-failure behavior applies to both code paths. File: .claude/hooks/tests/test_worktree_create.bats

description: Replace basename-based workspace name check with path-based detection

title: release-please config uses 'jj/skills/jujutsu' key instead of designed 'jj' key
description: Design doc (docs/plans/2026-03-07-jj-vcs-support-design.md section Task 9) specifies adding a top-level 'jj' package key to release-please-config.json and '.release-please-manifest.json'. Implementation uses 'jj/skills/jujutsu' as the key (with package-name: 'jj' to compensate) for consistency with the existing skill-directory-path convention used by other packages. A comment in release-please-config.json acknowledges this deviation. The functional outcome is equivalent, but it diverges from the stated design. No code change needed; if desired, update the design doc to reflect the actual convention used.

title: create_logging_jj_mock duplicates workspace-add path-extraction from create_mock_jj
description:
.claude/hooks/tests/helpers.bash: create_mock_jj (lines 14-44) and create_logging_jj_mock (lines 76-112) share identical path-extraction logic inside 'workspace add':

  shift 2
  for arg in "$@"; do
    case "$arg" in
      -*) ;;
      *) mkdir -p "$arg"; exit 0 ;;
    esac
  done
  exit 1

Both mock functions use this verbatim 8-line block. The only difference is that create_logging_jj_mock additionally writes to jj-args.log before the loop.

The mocks are written as heredocs inside shell functions, so extraction to a shared function isn't straightforward. However, the --help probe block is also duplicated across all five mock creators (create_mock_jj, create_old_jj_mock, create_always_failing_jj_mock, create_logging_jj_mock, create_failing_jj_mock).

Suggestion: consolidate the two full-featured mocks (create_mock_jj and create_logging_jj_mock) into a single create_mock_jj that accepts an optional LOG_ARGS parameter, or at minimum document the shared structure with a comment marking the identical sections to reduce maintenance cost.

title: VCS field parsing rules repeated in Phase 4 and Phase 5 of address-findings
description:
pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md explains VCS-discriminated field handling in two places:

Phase 4 step 5 (lines 280-290): Documents that fix-worker returns VCS, WORKTREE_BRANCH (git) or CHANGE_ID (jj), and gives inference fallback logic when VCS is missing.

Phase 5 (lines 465-474): Repeats the same VCS field discrimination rules for verification-runner output, but notes that CHANGE_ID/WORKTREE_BRANCH are informational there.

The Phase 5 block largely restates what Phase 4 already established. Since the verification-runner uses the same protocol, Phase 5 could reference Phase 4's rules rather than redefining them:

  Phase 5 (simplified): 'The verification-runner uses the same VCS field protocol as fix-worker (see Phase 4 step 5). These identifiers are informational — the primary result field is STATUS.'

This removes ~8 lines of repeated content.

title: collapse over-specified jj undo recovery prose in address-findings Phase 4b
description:
SKILL.md pr-review/skills/address-findings/SKILL.md lines 352-375 describe bookmark-set failure recovery with two separate 'jj undo' blocks, each with its own failure message and verification step. The structure is:

  1. Run jj undo  # revert failed bookmark set
     If fails: STOP with message A
  2. Run jj undo  # revert the rebase
     If fails: STOP with message B
  3. Verify log  # confirm pre-rebase state
     If fails: STOP with message C

This is ~24 lines covering an edge-within-an-edge (bookmark-set failure after rebase succeeds). The rebase failure path (lines 338-344) handles a similar undo in 7 lines. The bookmark-set section can be collapsed to match the same conciseness:

  If bookmark set fails:
  1. Run jj undo twice to revert both operations. If either undo fails, STOP and report manually.
  2. Verify: jj log -r @ --no-graph -n 1 — confirm pre-rebase state.
  3. Mark FAILED, re-queue.

This removes ~15 lines without losing information an agent needs to act on.
close_reason: Stale — the undo-twice prose was rewritten in commit 7730261 (ka4.59 fix). The new version is already concise with correct single-undo logic.

title: extract empty-parent cleanup to worktree-helpers.sh to eliminate duplication
description:
The logic to remove an empty worktree parent directory is duplicated in two places:

1. worktree-create.sh lines 31-33 (inside cleanup_on_error):
   if [[ -d WORKTREE_PARENT ]] && [[ -z $(ls -A WORKTREE_PARENT) ]]; then
     rmdir WORKTREE_PARENT 2>/dev/null || echo WARNING >&2
   fi

2. worktree-remove.sh lines 83-85:
   PARENT=$(dirname WORKTREE_PATH)
   if [[ -d PARENT ]] && [[ -z $(ls -A PARENT) ]]; then
     rmdir PARENT 2>/dev/null || echo WARNING >&2
   fi

Both implement the same pattern: if directory exists and is empty, try rmdir and warn on failure.

Suggested fix: add a helper to worktree-helpers.sh:

  remove_empty_dir() {
    local dir="$1"
    [[ -d "$dir" ]] || return 0
    [[ -z "$(ls -A "$dir")" ]] || return 0
    rmdir "$dir" 2>/dev/null || echo "WARNING: failed to remove empty directory '$dir'" >&2
  }

Then call remove_empty_dir in both scripts.

title: vcs-equivalence.md Workspace identity command identifies change, not workspace
description:
In pr-review/references/vcs-equivalence.md line 33, the 'Workspace identity' row maps to:
  jj: 'jj log -r @ --no-graph -T 'change_id.short(8) ++ " " ++ description.first_line()''

This command shows the CURRENT CHANGE identity (what @ is), not the WORKSPACE identity. The git equivalent 'git branch --show-current' shows which branch/workspace you are on.

For true workspace identity (analogous to git branch --show-current), the correct command is:
  basename "$(pwd)"   — since workspace name is the last path component for sibling worktrees
or
  jj workspace list   — lists all workspaces with paths

The current command can mislead agents into thinking they've confirmed workspace identity when they've only confirmed what change is checked out. An agent could be in the wrong workspace but on the right change and not detect it.

Suggestion: Either rename the row to 'Current change' (more accurate) or replace the jj command with 'basename "$(pwd)" && jj workspace list' to actually verify workspace identity. The SKILL.md Quick Reference table (line 301) has the same command under 'Current location' which is a more accurate label.

title: jj git push --change auto-bookmark name format is undocumented and version-dependent
description:
In jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md line 233, the comment states: '# Push the current change (auto-generates a bookmark named push-<id>)'

The actual auto-generated bookmark name format for 'jj git push --change @' is 'push-<change-id-prefix>' where the prefix length is determined by jj's internal disambiguation logic. The format 'push-<id>' is slightly imprecise — the '<id>' part is a truncated change ID (not a commit ID or sequential number). While the description is close enough to be useful, readers may not realize this is the change_id prefix, not commit SHA or full change_id.

Additionally, the auto-bookmark behavior was introduced at a specific jj version and the exact prefix length is not guaranteed stable.

Suggestion: Update the comment to: '# Push the current change (auto-creates a bookmark named push-<change-id>, e.g. push-kkmpptxz)' to clarify the ID type and give a concrete example format matching jj's actual output.

title: validate_safe_name trailing-dot rule has no test
description: worktree-helpers.sh validate_safe_name rejects names matching *. (trailing dot) via the regex branch on line 16. None of the test cases in test_worktree_create.bats or test_worktree_remove.bats exercise a name like 'name.' — the existing tests cover leading dots (.hidden), double-dots (..), and path traversal (../evil) but not the trailing-dot case. A simple test like: echo '{"name": "bad."}' | worktree-create.sh and asserting status=1 + 'invalid worktree name' would close this branch.

title: pure jj repo (no .git) not covered by standard mock jj tests
description: setup_jj() in test_worktree_create.bats only adds .jj/ to a git repo (setup() calls git init). This means detect_repo_root() takes the git rev-parse path — the jj root fallback branch (worktree-helpers.sh lines 35-41) is exercised only by the two inline-mock tests ('detect_repo_root falls back to jj root'). The main jj code path tests (jj path: creates workspace, forwards --name, cleans up on failure, etc.) never actually exercise detect_repo_root via jj root — they all go through git rev-parse. A test using a truly non-colocated jj repo (mktemp dir with .jj/ but no .git/) using create_mock_jj would close this gap and confirm the full jj-only flow end-to-end.

title: lefthook integration tests do not exercise jj code path
description: The three lefthook tests in test_worktree_create.bats (runs lefthook install when lefthook.yml exists, warns when lefthook install fails, skips lefthook install when absent) all use the default setup() which creates a plain git repo without .jj/. The lefthook block in worktree-create.sh is post-branch and shared, but since jj repos require different VCS detection it is worth having at least one test that calls setup_jj() + create_mock_jj() before running the lefthook path, to confirm the lefthook section is reached and works for jj repos. This would also catch any future refactor that accidentally gates lefthook behind the git branch.

title: worktree-remove.sh silently exits 0 when worktree path is non-empty but directory missing
description:
File: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh, lines 13-15

The guard at the top of the script:
  if [[ -z "$WORKTREE_PATH" || ! -d "$WORKTREE_PATH" ]]; then
    exit 0
  fi

exits 0 silently when WORKTREE_PATH is non-empty but the directory does not exist. This conflates two distinct cases: (1) no path provided (legitimate no-op) and (2) path provided but directory already gone (may indicate prior partial cleanup or a path typo). Case 2 should still attempt jj workspace forget / git worktree prune to clean up VCS metadata, since the directory can be gone while workspace metadata persists.

Suggested fix: Split the guard:
  if [[ -z "$WORKTREE_PATH" ]]; then exit 0; fi
  # If directory is gone, still attempt VCS metadata cleanup, then exit 0

title: jj path missing rm-rf failure test in worktree-remove
description: test_worktree_remove.bats has a test at line 201 that verifies the git path emits 'failed to remove worktree directory' when rm -rf fails (chmod a-w on parent). The jj code path uses the same rm -rf call (worktree-remove.sh line 77) but no equivalent test exists for the jj setup. setup_jj_worktree() creates a plain directory — the right starting point — but the permission-failure scenario is never exercised for jj repos. Suggested addition: a test in the jj code path block that calls setup_jj_worktree + create_mock_jj, chmod a-w on the _worktrees parent, and asserts status=1 with 'failed to remove worktree directory' in output.

title: GitHub Actions use mutable version tags without SHA pinning
description: The new bats CI job added in .github/workflows/check-skills.yml uses actions/checkout@v4 (mutable tag). The existing drift-check and lint jobs also use @v4 (corrected in this PR from the invalid @v6). The release-please.yml uses googleapis/release-please-action@v4. None are pinned to a commit SHA, so a compromised tag could run arbitrary code in CI. Pin to SHA: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 (v4.2.2). Note: this pattern pre-existed in the repo; the new bats job adds three more unpinned uses.

title: Unsanitized path echoed to stderr before validation in worktree-remove.sh
description: In worktree-remove.sh lines 20 and 24, the raw unsanitized input path (_ORIG_PATH from jq-parsed JSON) is echoed to stderr before validate_safe_name runs. ANSI escape sequences or control characters in the path reach Claude error logs unstripped. Fix: wrap with sanitize_for_output() before echoing. Impact: stderr only, no external trust boundary crossed.

title: Current location command differs between SKILL.md and vcs-equivalence.md
description:
Two documents in the PR document the 'current location' command for jj with different templates:

SKILL.md Quick Reference (line 298):
  jj log -r @ --no-graph -T 'change_id.short(8)'

vcs-equivalence.md Workspace identity row (line 34):
  jj log -r @ --no-graph -T 'change_id.short(8) ++ " " ++ description.first_line()'

Both are valid commands but they return different output -- the vcs-equivalence.md version includes the commit description while SKILL.md omits it. Agents reading one document will produce output formatted differently than agents reading the other.

Suggested fix: Use the same template in both documents. The vcs-equivalence.md version is more informative for human readability while the SKILL.md version produces cleaner parseable output. Pick one and note in the other that the forms differ for clarity.

title: SKILL.md describes jj absorb as equivalent to git absorb but git absorb is a third-party tool
description:
jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md line 143 states: 'This is the jj equivalent of git absorb -- it automatically routes each hunk to the right ancestor commit.'

git absorb is not a built-in git command -- it is a third-party tool (tummychow/git-absorb) that many developers may not have installed. Describing jj absorb as 'the jj equivalent of git absorb' may confuse readers unfamiliar with git-absorb, and implies git has a built-in absorb feature when it does not.

Additionally, 'right ancestor commit' is slightly imprecise -- jj absorb routes hunks to the closest mutable ancestor, not any ancestor. The actual jj help text says 'the closest mutable ancestor where the corresponding lines were modified last.'

Suggested fix: Clarify that git absorb is a third-party tool, or remove the comparison and simply describe what jj absorb does. Also consider adding 'mutable' before 'ancestor' for accuracy.

title: SKILL.md and jj-git-interop.md use inconsistent syntax for jj bookmark create
description:
jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md (line ~170) shows 'jj bookmark create <name>' without -r flag. jj/skills/jujutsu/references/jj-git-interop.md (line 18) shows 'jj bookmark create <name> -r @' with explicit -r flag.

Both forms are technically correct (jj bookmark create defaults -r to @), but the inconsistency between the two reference documents in the same skill creates unnecessary confusion for readers wondering which form to use.

Suggested fix: Pick one canonical form and use it consistently across both files. The explicit '-r @' form in jj-git-interop.md is more transparent (makes the target explicit), but the short form in SKILL.md matches how most jj documentation is written. Either way, a note that '-r @' is the default would clarify why both forms are valid.

title: test
description: test

title: .beads/metadata.json missing trailing newline and has redundant database/backend fields
description: Two issues in .beads/metadata.json. First: missing trailing newline -- the file ends with '}' (0x7d) with no newline, which triggers git diff noise (diff shows: No newline at end of file). Second: both 'database' and 'backend' fields are present with identical values ('dolt'). This dual-field schema encodes no additional information and 'database' appears to be a legacy alias for 'backend'. Without documentation, consumers cannot determine which field is authoritative when they diverge. Location: .beads/metadata.json lines 1-7. Suggested fixes: (1) Add trailing newline. (2) If 'database' is a beads-generated legacy field that cannot be removed without breaking bd, document this in a comment; otherwise remove the redundant field.

title: Behavioral evals B-JJ1 and B-JJ4 embed context in prompt string instead of context field
description: The eval schema supports an optional 'context' field for specifying environmental preconditions separately from the prompt (used correctly in B-VCS1, and jj T6/T8). However, B-JJ1 and B-JJ4 embed context inline in the prompt string using parenthetical notation. B-JJ1 prompt: 'address the review findings for PR #99 (in a repo with .jj/ directory)'. B-JJ4 prompt ends with '(in a repo with .jj/ directory)'. The inconsistency means tooling cannot programmatically distinguish prompt text from context setup. B-VCS1 in the same file correctly uses the 'context' field for the equivalent information. Location: pr-review/evals/evals.json -- B-JJ1 and B-JJ4 prompt fields. Suggested fix: Extract the parenthetical to a dedicated 'context' field, matching the pattern used by B-VCS1 and jj evals T6/T8.

title: Eval IDs non-unique across plugins: jj and pr-review share T1-T8 and B1-B6
description: jj/evals/evals.json reuses the same ID values (T1-T8, B1-B9) as pr-review/evals/evals.json. Each file is scoped to its own plugin, so IDs are locally unique, but any tooling that aggregates evals across plugins will produce ID collisions. The pr-review eval file already introduced globally-scoped IDs for cross-cutting evals ('B-JJ1', 'B-VCS1') showing awareness of this issue, but the jj plugin's own evals use the generic T/B scheme. Location: jj/evals/evals.json -- all 17 eval IDs (T1-T8, B1-B9) collide with pr-review IDs. Suggested fix: Prefix jj plugin eval IDs with a plugin-specific namespace, e.g., 'JJ-T1', 'JJ-B1'. This mirrors the pattern already established for cross-cutting evals ('B-JJ1', 'B-VCS1') in pr-review/evals/evals.json.

title: Eval schema: assertion type enum diverges between jj and pr-review evals
description: The two evals.json files use divergent assertion type enumerations with no shared schema reference. jj/evals/evals.json introduces 'command_invoked' and 'skill_not_triggered' types that do not appear in pr-review/evals/evals.json. Conversely, pr-review uses 'output_not_contains' which jj does not use. Since both files are parsed by the same eval runner, the type field is effectively an open string union with no schema document defining valid values. Location: jj/evals/evals.json (all assertion entries), pr-review/evals/evals.json (all assertion entries). jj-only types: command_invoked, skill_not_triggered. pr-review-only types: output_not_contains. Suggested fix: Add a companion schema file (e.g., evals.schema.json at repo root) defining the complete valid enum for assertion 'type' values, and ensure both files conform to it. Alternatively define the enum in a shared JSON Schema referenced via a dollar-schema key.

title: jj workspace forget on remove is a warning-only path, but silent failure leaves orphaned metadata
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh (lines 55-59), when jj is not installed the script emits a WARNING and continues. This correctly removes the physical directory, but leaves orphaned workspace metadata in jj (the workspace is still registered in jj but its files are gone).

The script already includes a manual recovery hint in the warning message, which is good. However, the BATS test 'jj path: warns but still removes directory when jj not installed' (test_worktree_remove.bats line 142) only verifies the WARNING is printed and directory is removed, without verifying the workspace metadata state.

This is an inherent operational constraint (can't forget a workspace without jj), and the warning adequately documents the situation. The finding is that the test could be improved to explicitly assert the warning contains actionable recovery instructions.

File: .claude/hooks/worktree-remove.sh, lines 55-59

title: create_logging_jj_mock hardcodes path as $3, fragile if arg order changes
description:
In .claude/hooks/tests/helpers.bash, the create_logging_jj_mock function (line 86) does mkdir -p "$3" — hardcoding the assumption that the destination path is the third argument to 'jj workspace add'. This currently matches the call site in worktree-create.sh which passes 'jj workspace add "$WORKTREE_PATH" --name ...'.

However, create_mock_jj (the regular mock, lines 24-30) correctly iterates args to find the first non-flag argument, which is more robust. If the argument order in worktree-create.sh ever changes (e.g., flags moved before the path), create_logging_jj_mock would silently create the wrong directory and tests would pass incorrectly.

Suggested fix: use the same flag-skipping loop pattern as create_mock_jj instead of hardcoding $3.

File: .claude/hooks/tests/helpers.bash, line 86

title: jj plugin.json missing required 'skills' and 'agents' manifest fields present in homelab/plugin.json and pr-review/plugin.json
description:
The new jj/plugin.json contains only three fields: name, version, description. Compare with pr-review/plugin.json (which includes skills[], agents[], commands[] arrays) and homelab/plugin.json (which includes skills[]). The jj plugin has at least one skill (jujutsu) and one command (jj-init). If the plugin manifest schema requires listing skills and commands for discovery, the jj plugin will fail to expose those to the Claude marketplace runtime.

Current jj/plugin.json:
{
  "name": "jj",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Jujutsu (jj) VCS workflow guidance for colocated repos"
}

No skills[] array listing jj/skills/jujutsu.
No commands[] array listing jj/commands/jj-init.md.

This may or may not be required by the marketplace schema (the schema URL https://anthropic.com/claude-code/marketplace.schema.json is not accessible to verify), but the inconsistency with the other plugins is notable.

Files: jj/plugin.json, jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md, jj/commands/jj-init.md
Suggested fix: Check homelab/plugin.json and pr-review/plugin.json structure, then add matching skills[] and commands[] arrays to jj/plugin.json.

title: Workspaces section is duplicated between SKILL.md and jj-git-interop.md
description:
Files:
  - jj/skills/jujutsu/SKILL.md lines 193-218 (Workspaces section)
  - jj/skills/jujutsu/references/jj-git-interop.md lines 51-65 (Workspaces section)

Both files contain a Workspaces section covering the same commands (jj workspace add, list, forget) and the same key behaviors (own working-copy commit per workspace, shared storage, changes visible across workspaces).

The SKILL.md version is more complete and includes jj workspace update-stale. The interop reference version is a subset. Any update to one must be mirrored in the other.

Suggested fix: Remove the Workspaces section from jj-git-interop.md and add a cross-reference: 'See SKILL.md for workspace commands.' The interop doc's purpose is to explain git/jj coexistence, not to duplicate workspace documentation.

title: validate_safe_name silently accepts empty string
description:
In .claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh, the validate_safe_name function does not reject an empty string. The regex [^a-zA-Z0-9_.-] does not match empty, and none of the glob conditions (.*, *"..", *.) match empty either. An empty name silently passes the validator.

In practice this is not exploitable: worktree-create.sh guards with an explicit empty check before calling validate_safe_name (line 14-17), and worktree-remove.sh derives the name from basename of a realpath-canonicalized directory path which cannot be empty. However, the validator is a reusable helper and its contract is misleading — callers that add new code paths without the pre-existing guard could introduce a regression.

Suggested fix: add an empty-string check as the first condition in validate_safe_name:
  if [[ -z "$name" || "$name" =~ [^a-zA-Z0-9_.-] || ... ]]

File: .claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh, line 6

title: create_logging_jj_mock in helpers.bash uses positional $3 for path instead of argument scan
description:
File: .claude/hooks/tests/helpers.bash, lines 84-87

create_logging_jj_mock uses $3 (the third positional arg after 'workspace add') as the destination path for mkdir-p. The actual call from worktree-create.sh is:

  jj workspace add "$WORKTREE_PATH" --name "worktree-${NAME}"

So $3 = WORKTREE_PATH, which is correct NOW. But this is fragile: if the argument order changes (e.g., --name moved before the path), the mock breaks silently — mkdir creates the wrong directory and the test still passes.

By contrast, create_mock_jj uses a robust loop that skips flags and finds the first non-flag arg (the correct approach used in the production hook itself).

create_logging_jj_mock should use the same flag-skipping loop that create_mock_jj uses, rather than hardcoding positional $3:

  shift 2
  dest=""
  for arg in "$@"; do
    case "$arg" in -*) ;; *) dest="$arg"; break ;; esac
  done
  echo "$@" > "${REPO_ROOT}/jj-args.log"
  mkdir -p "$dest"
  exit 0

This also aligns the two mocks' path-finding logic, reducing the chance of silent test failures from argument order changes.

title: check-skills.yml downgrades actions/checkout from v6 to v4 without explanation
description:
In .github/workflows/check-skills.yml, the actions/checkout version was changed from v6 to v4. This is a version downgrade of a CI workflow action, not an upgrade. The diff shows:

  -      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
  +      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

No comment or explanation is provided for this downgrade. v6 of actions/checkout may be a valid future release, or this could indicate the original file had a typo (v6 doesn't exist as of 2026). If v6 was intentional and valid, the downgrade may cause behavioral differences (e.g., different git fetch depth, auth token handling). If it was a typo that should have been v4 all along, this PR silently fixes it.

The new bats job added in the same diff correctly uses v4.

File: .github/workflows/check-skills.yml
Suggested action: Verify that v4 is correct and add a comment explaining the downgrade from v6 if warranted, or confirm v6 was a pre-existing typo.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: actions/checkout@v6 does not exist (latest is v4). The original v6 was a typo; this PR correctly fixes it to v4.

title: jj-init: .gitignore append failure is silent with no user feedback
description:
In jj/commands/jj-init.md, step 4 uses:
  grep -qxF '.jj/' .gitignore 2>/dev/null || echo '.jj/' >> .gitignore

This instructs Claude to run a command where, if echo >> .gitignore fails (e.g., read-only filesystem, permission denied), no error is reported to the user and the command is described as complete. The next step (step 5) runs 'jj st' and declares success without verifying .gitignore was written correctly.

Since this is a slash command that instructs Claude to execute shell commands rather than shell code Claude writes, the failure surface is limited. However, the standard for user-visible workflows should be that a write failure is reported.

File: jj/commands/jj-init.md, step 4

Suggested fix: Either wrap in an explicit check:
  echo '.jj/' >> .gitignore || { echo 'WARNING: failed to update .gitignore — add .jj/ manually'; }
Or in the instruction prose, add: 'If the append fails, report the error and tell the user to add .jj/ to .gitignore manually.'

title: worktree-helpers.sh helper file not specified in either design document
description: Both design documents (docs/plans/2026-03-06-worktree-isolation-fix-design.md and docs/plans/2026-03-07-jj-vcs-support-design.md) list specific files changed. Neither mentions .claude/hooks/worktree-helpers.sh. The implementation adds this helper file with validate_safe_name and detect_repo_root shared between worktree-create.sh and worktree-remove.sh. This is a good engineering practice (DRY, security hardening via input validation), but it is an undocumented addition relative to the spec. The Files Changed table in the worktree isolation design doc should be updated to include this file, or a brief note added explaining the factoring decision.

title: vcs-detection-preamble.md is a third reference file not in the design spec's two-file scope
description: Design doc Section 4 (docs/plans/2026-03-07-jj-vcs-support-design.md) states explicitly: 'Two reference files, each consumed by different skills' — vcs-equivalence.md and jj-git-interop.md. The implementation adds a third reference file pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md that extracts the VCS detection startup procedure into a shared document consumed by all 12 agents and 3 skills. This is a beneficial refactoring (DRY, single source of truth), but the design doc's 'Two reference files' count is now incorrect and CLAUDE.md/memory does not mention this third file's role. Suggested fix: Update the 'Reference Files' table in docs/plans/2026-03-07-jj-vcs-support-design.md to add the third entry, or add a brief note that the preamble was extracted to a separate file during implementation.

title: vcs-equivalence.md has two near-identical rows mapping git branch --show-current
description:
File: pr-review/references/vcs-equivalence.md, lines 23 and 34

Two table rows map the identical git command (git branch --show-current) to jj equivalents with slightly different output templates:

  Line 23: | Current location | git branch --show-current | jj log -r @ --no-graph -T 'change_id.short(8)' |
  Line 34: | Workspace identity | git branch --show-current | jj log -r @ --no-graph -T 'change_id.short(8) ++ " " ++ description.first_line()' |

This is confusing: readers see the same git command appearing twice mapping to different jj commands. Options to resolve:
1. Merge into one row using the longer template (strictly more informative).
2. Rename to clarify the distinction: e.g. 'Terse change ID' vs 'Change ID + description'.
3. Drop the shorter form, since the verbose form covers both use cases.

The current duplication is likely to cause agents to pick arbitrarily between the two templates.

title: vcs-detection-preamble: jj workspace list failure produces silent no-op
description:
In pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md, step 2 for jj VCS instructs agents to run 'jj workspace list' and parse the output to find the current workspace name. There is no guidance on what to do if 'jj workspace list' itself fails (e.g., jj not in PATH, jj internal error, or invoked outside a jj repo despite .jj/ existing).

If the command fails, an agent parsing no output would find no line ending with '(current)', so the workspace name extraction step produces an empty string. The preamble then says 'If you are in the default workspace, STOP' — but an empty-string workspace name does not match 'default', so the agent would proceed past the guard with an unverified location.

File: pr-review/references/vcs-detection-preamble.md, step 2 (jj verification)

Suggested fix: Add an explicit failure case:
  'If jj workspace list fails or produces no output, STOP and report
  STATUS: FAILED -- "jj workspace list failed: <error>"'

title: release-please-manifest.json key uses jj/skills/jujutsu instead of jj as specified
description: Design doc (docs/plans/2026-03-07-jj-vcs-support.md Task 9, Step 2) specifies adding '"jj": "0.1.0"' to .release-please-manifest.json. The implementation uses '"jj/skills/jujutsu": "0.1.0"' to match the release-please-config.json package key. The implementation is internally consistent and the config has a self-documenting comment explaining the deviation. However, the manifest key differs from what the design spec wrote. The deviation is benign — release-please requires the manifest key to match the config key — but it is undocumented relative to the spec. No action required unless strict spec tracking is needed; the deviation is justified.

comments.author: seanb4t
comments.text: Closed: deviation is justified. release-please requires manifest key to match config key. The implementation is internally consistent and self-documenting.

title: mkdir -p WORKTREE_PARENT duplicated in both jj and git branches of worktree-create.sh
description:
File: .claude/hooks/worktree-create.sh, lines 55-77

mkdir -p WORKTREE_PARENT is duplicated in both branches of the if/else (jj path line 55, git path line 68), along with a duplicated error message. Since both branches always need the directory, it could be hoisted before the if/else after cleanup_on_error is defined.

Before:
  if [[ -d "${REPO_ROOT}/.jj" ]]; then
    ...
    mkdir -p "$WORKTREE_PARENT" || { echo "ERROR: ..."; exit 1; }
    ...
  else
    mkdir -p "$WORKTREE_PARENT" || { echo "ERROR: ..."; exit 1; }
    ...
  fi

After:
  mkdir -p "$WORKTREE_PARENT" || {
    echo "ERROR: failed to create worktree parent directory '$WORKTREE_PARENT'" >&2
    exit 1
  }
  if [[ -d "${REPO_ROOT}/.jj" ]]; then
    ...
  else
    ...
  fi

This removes ~5 lines and one duplicated error message without changing behavior.

title: check-skills.yml: rumdl binary downloaded without version-pinned checksum verification on install step
description:
In .github/workflows/check-skills.yml lines 58-65, the rumdl binary is downloaded via 'gh release download' with a sha256 checksum file. The checksum is verified with 'sha256sum -c'. This is a good practice.

However, the RUMDL_VERSION is hardcoded as 'v0.1.43'. The workflow uses actions/checkout@v4 without a pinned SHA. While this is common practice, pinning third-party actions to a full commit SHA (e.g., actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683) is the supply-chain security best practice per GitHub's hardening guide, since a tag like @v4 can be force-pushed to point to a different commit.
